programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A crook's attempt to blackmail a toxic waste company does not go according to plan, but reunites Dr Sloan with his daughter for the first time in eight years. Dick Van Dyke's real-life daughter Stacy guest stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A photograph draws a colleague of Dr Sloan into a blackmail plot linked to the murder of a Hollywood madam. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan's vivid memory comes in handy during an investigation into a 30-year-old case involving a missing person and embezzled funds. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: As they await the verdict of Cabe's trial, the team tries to focus their energy on rescuing a diabetic dog trapped underground

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: On Christmas Eve, after Walter chooses work over spending time with his friends, he hits his head and enters a dream world where Team Scorpion never existed

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Navy Public Affairs Officer is killed, and when the team takes up the case, both the man's wife and his mistress emerge as potential suspects. Each woman offering a very different take on the chain of events that led to the murder, and the investigators are forced to decide which of the two is telling the truth. Meanwhile, DiNozzo's father comes to the aid of a homeless woman, who makes a surprising claim about his relationship with her. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony finds himself in a difficult position when his identity is stolen. Worse yet, multiple fraudsters make use of his status as a special agent to blackmail US senators, and as the chain of events begins to spiral out of control, it is left to the real Dinozzo to bring the perpetrators to justice. Naval crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs teams up with his old friend Senior FBI Agent TC Fornell after two British prisoners, including a former spy, escape captivity and flee to the US via a shipping container. Meanwhile, McGee is busy hunting for a new apartment, and is determined to discover how DiNozzo managed to snag his lavish home. Naval crime drama, guest starring Joe Spano

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A 14-year-old boy manages to foil a home invasion, but when Gibbs is called in to investigate, he suspects the teenager may be withholding crucial information. As the case makes progress, Gibbs turns his attention to helping the boy's mother, the wife of a naval officer, and his efforts result in an invitation to the White House to meet the First Lady (Michelle Obama, guest starring as herself). Meanwhile, Director Vance and FBI Agent Fornell travel to London as part of the ongoing case involving the escaped British spies

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team, alongside the FBI and MI6, continues the international manhunt for a disgraced British spy who is seeking revenge on the agents he blames for his wife's death. A re-examination of the fugitive's conviction for selling military intelligence reveals that he may have been framed - and the identity of the only person who can clear his name

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: As they await the verdict of Cabe's trial, the team tries to focus their energy on rescuing a diabetic dog trapped underground

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: On Christmas Eve, after Walter chooses work over spending time with his friends, he hits his head and enters a dream world where Team Scorpion never existed

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The FBI's domestic terrorist task force, including operational psychologist Nate Getz, steps in to help the team when a former marine is suspected of creating a chemical bomb. Crime drama, starring Peter Cambor and Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part two of two. The teams fly to Los Angeles from Hawaii in pursuit of suspect Jarrod Taylor, before tracking him down at his hotel, where he claims he is being paid by an immunologist who is hoping to reintroduce smallpox to restore nature's balance. Later, the agents make a shocking discovery when they find a bulk order for children's t-shirts. Concluding part of the crossover episode with Hawaii Five-0

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Kensi and Deeks go undercover as a married couple in a quiet suburb as part of an investigation into a Russian sleeper cell. Crime drama, starring Daniela Ruah and Eric Christian Olsen

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part one of two. The agents investigate when two men are killed at a gun shop, but soon discover one of their colleagues could be at risk. Callen learns the Chameleon - a criminal out for revenge - is involved, and races to track him down. However, when he comes face to face with the fugitive, he is tricked into answering a phone wired to a car bomb

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Chameleon's planted clues lead the team on a wild goose chase as it searches for NSA agent Mike Atley, who the criminal claims to have in his custody. Callen is faced with a difficult decision when he is told to release the Chameleon in order to save Atley's life, and contemplates subduing his nemesis once and for all

programme.desc: Jack gets closer to a conman friend who is trying to straighten out his life, but a fatal car crash complicates matters and an investigation ensues. The case leads to a luxury health spa, where Dr Sloan seizes the opportunity to operate undercover

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A prison doctor is held hostage by one of Sloan's patients - a convicted criminal demanding to see the newscaster who covered his trial. Drama, starring Dick Van Dyke and Scott Baio

programme.desc: Kimble saves the life of Maggie Tippet, the one-armed man's girlfriend

programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.desc: Mac, Danny and Lindsay investigate the death of a doll-shop owner found buried beneath his dismantled stock. Meanwhile, Stella and Dr Hawkes learn a waitress has been killed in a new flat given to her by a customer. Detective drama, with Gary Sinise

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Ziva's family farm in Israel is devastated by an explosion, and the former NCIS agent is reported dead. Determined to seek justice, Tony ignores orders to stay off the case and sets out to track down Trent Kort, the British national and ex-CIA agent who remains the prime suspect

Drama, with Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The ranger gets involved when a vengeful group of police officers are revealed to be murdering criminals who are set free on technicalities. Adventure with the modern-day lawman whose crime-solving methods are rooted firmly in the Old West

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The investigators examine the death of US marine Tommy Hill, who appears to have been fatally stabbed en route to a homecoming party thrown in his honour by his foster family. Meanwhile, former operations manager Hetty, who quit over her colleague's actions, is adjusting to life in retirement. Crime drama set in the high-stakes world of the Office of Special Projects, starring Chris O'Donnell, LL Cool J and Linda Hunt

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A petty officer is killed in what initially seems to be a road-rage incident - until investigations reveal evidence of foul play. programme.desc: US Navy lieutenant Paul Booth is shot dead while sitting on a park bench. It turns out that the last people he called were his boss, his wife and Penelope Langston - a rebellious academic who also happens to be McGee's grandmother. She says that the victim was an old student of hers who kept in touch, but later reveals she was recruited to work on a special project during the Vietnam War by the head of a top-secret think tank Booth was a member of. Guest starring Lily Tomlin, with Mark Harmon and Sean Murray

Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Barney Hussein immediately becomes prime suspect when he is seen standing over the unconscious body of a woman in an alleyway and dropping a stun gun as he makes his escape. The confused victim reluctantly reveals a completely different train of events leading to the attack involving a flirtation with stripper Tom Norris. Will impresses Terry on his first day as a trainee Detective Constable with CID

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A joyrider makes her escape, leaving Millie and Nate to discover a body in the boot of the abandoned car. CSE Eddie Olosunje reveals the victim has been sexually assaulted and the trail of evidence leads back to stripper Tom Norris, who is already under suspicion for the attack on Paula Merrick. Sam arrests Norris and tries to provoke a reaction from him, but fears the court case is going to be long and emotionally draining

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Posing as a dockyard worker, Max sets up the arrest of Pete Monks and Tom Harris for stealing DVD players, but the operation is held up by Intelligence Officer Karen Lacy. She reveals the Monks family have been under surveillance for months and a big consignment of cocaine is due to arrive at any time, providing a unique opportunity to nail crime kingpin Dave Monks (played by guest star Shaun Williamson). Concludes tomorrow

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gangster Dave Monks outwits the police and his own cronies by diverting the delivery of cocaine from a docking ship, sending Max on a dummy run in the van. The thug vents his anger on his new barmaid Beth after his daughter Chloe rings to say she is breaking off all contact, and it is only a matter of time before his son Peter works out Max is not who he claims to be. Shaun Williamson and Liz May Brice guest star

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Trixie is chuffed when local curate Tom invites her to join him for a day of cricket, sending her to work with a spring in her step. Is love on the cards for the young midwife? Shelagh's infertility has made her think long and hard about what she wants in life, so she approaches her husband with an idea, while Patsy upsets a young mother in the clinic and is moved to the district rota, where she works tirelessly to diagnose a docker's unusual symptoms

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Cynthia is concerned during a visit as she feels new mum Pamela is overly anxious, so Dr Turner arranges for a psychiatrist to come in. However, they soon discover both mother and baby have gone missing. Meanwhile, Jenny, on secondment, begins to see cracks in the establishment, Chummy discovers her mother has walked out on her father, and Trixie struggles to hide her disappointment when Tom cancels a date. Guest starring Sophie Rundle (The Bletchley Circle)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Jenny is assigned to first-time mother Jeanette, who requires frequent visits during a protracted labour, and when she meets her patient's cousin, Philip, a new friendship begins to grow for the midwife. Meanwhile, Chummy worries about her mother's illness when she discovers Lady Browne has discharged herself from hospital. Jessica Raine and Miranda Hart star

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's Colour Blind
programme.desc: Drama from the best-selling author. Set between the wars, it concerns a girl who returns to her Tyneside home newly married to her black husband, only to encounter bigotry from all sides, to say nothing of her obsessed brother's animosity. Niamh Cusack, Tony Armatrading and Dearbhla Molloy star

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Dr Turner is reunited with a couple whose daughter was born without limbs, and takes them to a specialist unit where she can be fitted for prosthetics. However, while the experience helps the girl's mother to realise that she is not alone, it proves traumatic for the father, who blames his wife for his child's condition. Nurse Crane takes charge of Sister Winifred's driving lessons, and Trixie thinks Christopher is hiding something from her

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. An unpopular colonel is found dead in the vicarage of St Mary Mead and investigations reveal no shortage of suspects - including the vicar and members of the victim's family. Christmas edition from 1986, starring Joan Hickson, Paul Eddington and James Hazeldine

programme.title: The Casual Vacancy
programme.desc: Drama adapted from the novel by JK Rowling, set in an English village whose seemingly idyllic facade hides a community at war. At the parish council meeting, chairman Howard Mollison introduces his plans to turn the community centre into a health spa, but he reckons without local solicitor Barry Fairbrother, who makes an impassioned speech about the importance of the centre to the impoverished people of the nearby estate. However, an unexpected turn of events leaves an empty seat on the council - leading to the biggest battle Pagford has ever seen. Michael Gambon, Rory Kinnear, Keeley Hawes and Julia McKenzie star

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: When Penny's (Hayley McElhinney) away on honeymoon Hugh (Rodger Corser) and Tara (Kate Jenkinson) come out to play, with a little help from Charlie (Nicole da Silva) their night takes a dramatic turn as Hugh loses more than his sobriety

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: April is pregnant, and not everyone is as supportive as Matt had hoped, but the netball game between the hospital and mining company takes all the focus

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detective struggles to cope following the death of his wife and suffers more grief when the body of his godson is discovered in a bricked-up drain 12 years after he went missing. Lynley himself becomes a suspect when the boy's sister is found dead, forcing him to enlist the help of his lawyer and DS McCaffrey's son to prove his innocence. Crime drama, starring Nathaniel Parker, Sharon Small and Nicholas Farrell

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Burke and the team investigate university lecturer and crime novelist Mark Joffe, whose debut publication includes the detailed description of a murder identical to that of a real-life unsolved case. As the case unfolds, Burke becomes convinced of the writer's guilt, but cannot seem to find sufficient evidence to charge him. Guest starring Finlay Robertson (Life Begins), with Alex Norton, Blythe Duff and John Michie

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Southampton v Fulham at St Mary's Stadium and Brighton & Hove Albion v West Ham United at Amex Stadium. Fulham and Brighton are still battling it out to avoid relegation to the Championship, while the Hammers were looking to move a step closer to securing a place in Europe. Plus, Burnley v Leeds United and the Friday night contest between Newcastle United and Manchester City

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Leicester City. Gabby Logan presents action from the final at Wembley Stadium, as the sides battled it out for the prestigious trophy. The Blues secured their place in the showpiece match with a 1-0 last-four victory over Manchester City, while Leicester overcame Southampton by the same scoreline

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: Andrew's guests are Matt Hancock MP, health secretary, and Secretary John Kerry, US special envoy on climate. With news review from Simon Jack, BBC business editor, and Kate Garraway, presenter of ITV's Good Morning Britain.

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: Sunday Morning Stories
programme.desc: Rev Kate Bottley hears from people who have given up their time to help others, including the UK's youngest chief executive and a couple who offered their home to a Syrian refugee

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Santiago
programme.desc: The seven celebrities continue their trek along the Camino de Santiago pilgrimage to their destination of the Cathedral of Santiago de Compostela. The Rev Kate Bottley discovers a fountain of free-flowing wine, while Ed Byrne meets a man who believes money is the root of all evil. As the group bonds strengthen, JJ Chalmers and Neil Morrissey open up about traumatic experiences that have shaped their lives, while Debbie McGee leaves a stone bearing the name of her late husband, Paul Daniels, at the foot of the cathedral's famous Iron Cross. Narrated by Lee Ingleby

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Raj Bisram help two teams at Southwell racecourse in Nottinghamshire make a profit at auction. Hosted by Anita Manning

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones explores the Cathedral town of Exeter and, in Dementia Action Week, YolanDa Brown meets one local woman helping people with dementia to remain active

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers have their say on the BBC's output and there are behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple in the Welsh county of Ceredigion, looking to buy a country home with the potential for a spiritual retreat on a budget of £355,000

programme.title: Cars 3 (2017)
programme.desc: Constantly outshined and outraced by a fleet of hi-tech rookies, former champ Lightning McQueen must find a new way to win. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Owen Wilson, Cristela Alonzo and Chris Cooper

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates footage of a lion pride on Kenya's Masai Mara that has reached a dangerous and unusual turning point where the future of the group hangs in the balance. The adult males that had defended the pride have all deserted the group, leaving two remaining adult females to protect their eight cubs from a wide range of threats, including rival prides, herds of buffalo and the constant menace of marauding hyenas. However, the greatest threat of all comes when the lions encounter humans

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith head to the Peak District to celebrate the 70th anniversary of the UK's first National Park. Matt joins a pioneering scheme to restore ruined farm buildings and meets 'legend' of the Peak District Gordon Miller, one of the area's early rangers and a last link with those who brought about the National Park. Charlotte channels her inner artist to help to create a giant anniversary art mural and records the special sounds of the Park's waterways, while Joe Crowley investigates new pollution laws causing controversy in the countryside

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The Roadshow is at Stonor Park in Oxfordshire, where finds include a rare group of Indian medals, Florence Nightingale's toolkit and the story of a remarkable female pilot from the Second World War. There is also an intriguing painting by an unknown artist, some computer generated art with a surprisingly high value and a collection of rare pearls from around the world

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne is concerned when a toddler displays troubling symptoms, while an overly protective mother struggles to cope with her daughter's teenage pregnancy and is insistent on keeping it low key, much to the frustration of new Pupil Midwife Nancy

programme.title: The Pursuit of Love
programme.desc: Fanny embraces motherhood and tries to live up to society's image of a good wife, while Linda throws herself into a lifestyle of all-night parties as her marriage to Tony almost immediately falls apart. When she is pulled away by an enigmatic stranger, she sees the chance to make a drastic change and start a whole new life

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including West Bromwich Albion v Liverpool at The Hawthorns. Sam Allardyce's men held the Reds to a 1-1 draw when the sides met at Anfield in December in what was the start of a downturn in form for Liverpool. Plus, Everton v Sheffield United, Crystal Palace v Aston Villa and Tottenham Hotspur v Wolverhampton Wanderers

programme.title: Once Were Lions
programme.desc: Documentary following the rugby league Great Britain Lions' tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea, as they fight for their sporting future

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Angela Rippon and Julia Somerville are back in the studio, investigating the latest scams doing the rounds and finding out how consumers can get a good deal while they are out and about

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Developers with a pressing deadline of their own race to refurbish a terraced house in Tylorstown in south Wales, a businesswoman buys a former takeaway to help her business, and a developer got more than he bargains for when he buys a piece of land on the Isle of Sheppey. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow progress as the auction buys are developed

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: A web of deceit from a greedy council tenant, and police foil a criminal gang running a sophisticated £8 million fake transport ticket fraud

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A farm engineer's new truck loses a wheel while he's driving home, and a five-year-old boy uses the number on his toy ambulance to call for help when his mum is taken ill

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Ochucko Ojiri help the teams to find bargains in Leominster, Herefordshire, and Christina Trevanion learns about oil painting restoration

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie gets some dating advice on her first day of full training as a healthcare assistant with Luca. Rob and Harriet attend an unusual crime scene, where a woman is found tied up in the boot of her own car

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Maureen, Matt, Marisa and Stefan vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple from Scotland look for a property in the Cotswolds on a budget of £600,000, and drops in on show jumping double Olympic gold medallist Nick Skelton

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is at Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey with experts Nell Wragg and Sarah Peterson, who restore an unwanted windsurf sail, a chest of drawers, and a child's cot

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Newbie Irita Marriott brings Latvian flair to the game as she takes on Tim Medhurst in a sporting tour of the antiques emporia of Lincolnshire. One expert has a brush with the law

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Molly is taken aback to learn about Bernie's surrogacy plan and asks her not to go through with it, for the sake of their relationship. Denise bumps into a familiar face down at the allotments, and Sharon tells Zack to stop bringing women back to the flat. Nancy encourages Mick to finish his victim statement and read it in court himself - but unknown to them, Katy is back in the Square

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: A loud-mouthed, abrasive American comes to stay at the hotel and soon exposes Basil's ignorance of transatlantic cuisine - particularly Waldorf salad. Classic comedy, starring John Cleese, Prunella Scales and Andrew Sachs

programme.title: The Pact
programme.desc: When a young brewery boss is found dead, a chain of events is triggered that draws four of his employees into a fragile pact of silence that will change their lives for ever. Anna and her friends Nancy, Louie and Cat work at the brewery under young boss Jack Evans, whose bullying, spite and manipulation has taken its toll. After too many drinks at a work function, the women make a snap decision to bundle Jack into a car and dump him in the woods as a prank - but soon after, Jack is found dead. Starring Laura Fraser, Eiry Thomas, Heledd Gwynn and Julie Hesmondhalgh

programme.title: Starstruck
programme.desc: Kate receives an invitation to Tom's premiere and forces a reluctant Jessie to tag along. Tom and Jessie are interrupted by an unexpected visitor

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Morgan is desperate to know if ex Josh wanted an open relationship. With the truth finally out could they have a future together? Tasheeka thought she'd found her perfect match in Dexter until he suddenly ghosted her. He's ready to reveal the truth but will it be too much to swallow? Childhood sweethearts Daniel and Scarlett were plain sailing until he left her to go travelling. Could there be any way back for these lovebirds?

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race Down Under
programme.desc: The Queens are split in two, as they compete in the ultimate Girl Group sing-off, as the quest to become Down Under's first Drag Race Superstar continues

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Heather Small, as they assist Caitlin and Kristen from Warrington in picking out the best singers from a line-up that includes a singing wrestler, a model and a dentist

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Julia Somerville and Gloria Hunniford take on the fraudsters leaving people thousands of pounds out of pocket, look at the big-name companies who have left some of their customers hanging and present expert advice to help make sure consumers get the best deal available

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin, Martel Maxwell and Lucy Alexander follow a four-year project to develop land in Maidstone, Kent, plus properties in Burnley and Meir, Staffordshire

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: The team investigate a wealthy manager who has been taking advantage of the NHS, and the million-pound tax fraudster who claimed to be a spy

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A teenager fights to save her father's life when he has a heart attack, and a driver loses control of a car, scooping two men up on to the bonnet and crashing through a shop window

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Caroline Hawley guide two teams scouring Spitalfields Market in the heart of London's East End for bargains to take to Catherine Southon's auction house. Plus, presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out how the area around Spitalfields became known as Weaver's Town with TV historian Dan Cruickshank

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid makes preparations for the big apple Sale, while Karen is excited by the prospect of another foster child. Luca gets involved with a patient undergoing superhero therapy

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Contestants Helena, Andy, Carly and Tony vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to win a cash prize. Quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A musical couple from London need Steve Brown's help to unearth a new home in Oxfordshire. On the £950,000 shopping list is a detached property with space for a studio and good links to the capital. Away from the search, Steve checks out a dairy farm where he samples kefir, purportedly an ancient, health-giving drink made from fermented milk

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades saves an old radio, a set of chairs and a side table and chest of drawers from being thrown in the tip, hoping he and two others can make money by restoring the items

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The antiques rivals arrive in Suffolk, where Tim Medhurst likes the look of an art nouveau piece, while Irita Marriott learns why the local cottages are pink

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Frankie is shocked to see Katy at the club and feels conflicted when her mother asks her for a character reference. Martin is annoyed by Zack's constant flirting with Ruby - and even more annoyed when Lily tells him the pair of them spent a lot of time together while he was away. Bernie is under pressure to come up with money for her family and a mystery man arrives in the Square looking for Sonia. Brian Conley joins the cast

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: A major incident strikes Holby and with pupils from Hanssen's old school fighting for life, Josh worries that the CEO is struggling to cope. Will he be brave enough to ask for help before it's too late? Secret addict Kian's focus is frayed when asked to choose between a patient and surrogate son Andrei, and inexperienced doctor Jeong is thrown in at the deep end when Dom asks him to diagnose a mystery condition

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate a scam in which a woman ended up £20,000 out of pocket after beginning an online relationship with a US Navy captain

programme.title: The Pact
programme.desc: As the police begin interviewing the brewery workers about Jack's background and his behaviour at the party, the women go to extraordinary lengths to protect their dark secret. However, someone else knows what happened that night and is now holding them to ransom. Drama, starring Laura Fraser, Julie Hesmondhalgh, Eiry Thomas and Heledd Gwynn, with Eddie Marsan and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the opening matches in the penultimate round of Premier League fixtures, including Chelsea v Leicester City at Stamford Bridge. Chelsea and the Foxes were facing each other for the second time in four days, having also met in the FA Cup final, and this contest could have proved to be just as important as the clubs jostled for positions inside the top four. Plus, Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester City, Manchester United v Fulham and Southampton v Leeds United

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The amateur make-up artists are booked for a photo shoot for online retailer ASOS. They are given two hours to showcase their mascara, but also feature a shimmery eye and a glow on the skin, all of which must align with the company's brand identity. Back in the studio, the amateurs create a look that is 'out of this world', before the two weakest face off in the elimination. Presented by Maya Jama

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norma turns to Romero for guidance when she finds herself in an unexpected position of power. As the pair push the limits with the powerful Bob Paris, Norman and Dylan both come undone at the fallout of a secret, while Caleb considers an offer made to him by a surprising source

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Emma grows closer to Dylan when they both go to Norman's aid. Meanwhile, Norma begins to consider a life away from White Pine Bay

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don plants out his tomatoes and shows viewers what they can feed their plants. Arit Anderson looks into the use of peat in the horticultural industry, and Sue Kent reveals the progress on her allotment

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison explore Cranborne Chase, help tend to Britain's biggest modern-day maze, and meet farmers who are working together to encourage wildlife on their land. Steve Brown goes stargazing, Tom Heap reports on the possible environmental costs of a new space race launched from rural sites, and Adam Henson takes his rare breed Suffolk Punch to the maternity unit

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Documentary highlighting how British Jews from across the faith celebrate life's big milestones, from birth to coming of age, marriage to the end of life. A 12-year-old prepares for his bar mitzvah, a couple marry in Spain, a modern ceremony they have adapted according to their beliefs, and at Bushey Cemetery in Hertfordshire, a woman describes her role as a volunteer who does the tahara, the ritual washing of the deceased before burial

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond and Nick Nairn cook up some wonderful local produce on the Black Isle. Arlene Stuart investigates the welfare issues associated with fast growing chickens, while Euan McIlwraith continues his series about his favourite birds, focusing on the fulmar. The programme also catches up with Polly Pullar, the conservationist who rehabilitates an array of birds and beasties

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Brian Cunningham reviews the success of Beechgrove's box-hedging alternatives, while he and Mairi Rattray also share topiary tips. Plus, catching up with the Seil Community Garden, which Beechgrove helped create in 2009.

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: The Great Northern Garden Build
programme.desc: Documentary following the creation of RHS Garden Bridgewater in Salford, one of the most ambitious and challenging gardening projects the UK has ever seen. The programme goes behind the scenes to meet the team of designers, engineers and gardeners taking on this colossal construction project on the site of a former grand Victorian estate in Greater Manchester

programme.title: Rick Stein's India
programme.desc: The chef arrives in the state of Rajasthan, a land of palaces and hilltop forts, where he cooks a biryani fit for a royal banquet. He then heads north to the mountainous region of Himachal Pradesh, and joins local nobility at a gathering in their home village and has an audience with the Dalai Lama

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from the south west of England go head to head in the last of the regional finals as they cook their six-course menus again. The judges are food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and food creative Rachel Khoo, as well as a guest judge, inventor Colin Furze. Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Live Diving
programme.desc: Live coverage of the final day of diving from the European Aquatics Championships in Budapest

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Celia Imrie looks at the careers of leading British actresses, including Emma Thompson, Diana Rigg, Julie Walters, Elizabeth Taylor, Maggie Smith, Judi Dench and Helen Mirren

programme.title: Tea with Mussolini (1999)
programme.desc: Eccentric English and American women living in Florence during the tense pre-war years of the 1930s experience the ups and downs of parenthood when they collectively adopt a local boy disowned by his father. However, as international hostilities break out, their happy existence comes under threat. Period comedy drama, starring Maggie Smith, Judi Dench, Joan Plowright, Cher, Lily Tomlin and Baird Wallace

programme.title: Live Diving
programme.desc: Further live coverage of the final day of diving from the European Aquatics Championships in Budapest, featuring the women's 3 metre synchro and the men's 10 metre platform finals

programme.title: Our Wild Adventures
programme.desc: BBC wildlife presenters reflect on their visits to the Arctic, with Liz Bonnin looking back on her time helping a team of scientists locate a population of rare Siberian tigers. Gordon Buchanan recounting the time he went to Ellesmere Island in Canada in search of wild wolves that had never seen humans before, and Chris Packham remembers the time he ventured to Greenland on an expedition to uncover the secret life of an iceberg

programme.title: Gods of Snooker
programme.desc: A look at the emergence of Steve Davis and the rise of promoter Barry Hearn's Matchroom group of players, which brought lucrative sponsorships into the game. By the mid-1980s, snooker was at the peak of its powers and in 1985 nearly 20 million people tuned in to see Steve Davis play Dennis Taylor in the World Championship final

programme.title: Davis v Taylor: The '85 Black Ball Final
programme.desc: Colin Murray presents a look back at the final of the 1985 World Snooker Championship between Dennis Taylor and Steve Davis. The match finished after midnight, but kept an estimated 18.5 million viewers engrossed until the title was decided on the final black of the 35th frame. With contributions from Barry McGuigan, Ted Lowe, Stephen Hendry and Barry Hearn

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: Franklin's troubles are affecting Cissy and Alton in their home, while Gustavo has sworn revenge against Fuentes, but his uncompromising methods jeopardise Teddy's operation

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: Even though he is now out on his own, Franklin's problems keep landing on Leon's doorstep, and he decides to handle it his own way. Teddy deals with the repercussions of his diversion in Mexico with Gustavo, while Irene is getting closer to something big

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Donna leads the Holby nursing staff in protest - will Hanssen be moved by their cause or are things about to get much worse for them? Lucky realises that Kian lied to her about where Andrei was staying, while an accidental encounter between Josh and Hanssen threatens to spill both of their secrets

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Jonathan Pratt are in Nottinghamshire to help two teams look for items of value at the Newark Antiques and Collectable fair. Presenter Charlie Ross hits the fair to see if anyone can help him with a mystery object and later in the show the team heads to Derby for the highs and lows of the auction

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is at Witley Recycling Centre, salvaging three unwanted gems so they can shine again. A broken display cabinet finds its way to furniture artist Chloe Kempster, designer Daniel Heath needs to set a 1970s dressing table back on the right path, and Sarah tries to help a chandelier gleam again

programme.title: Inside the Zoo
programme.desc: Edinburgh Zoo keeper Ceri must find a way to give medicine to a poorly tapir, while up north at the Highland Wildlife Park near Aviemore, Rachel, Louise and Judith brave the elements to construct a new climbing frame for the snow monkeys

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc in which pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords as they attempt to take home the £2,500 prize money

programme.title: Good Morning Dagenham
programme.desc: Dean has a big opportunity when market boss Frank takes a rare day off, while a newcomer arrives with an array of games he designed and built himself. Larry Lamb narrates

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Jack has a theory about who was responsible for the accident that put Henry's career in doubt. Anna returns to Ash Park and reveals the identity of the father of her unborn child

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The quarter-finalists prepare for another two challenges but first there's a surprise as, for one week only, Michelin-starred chef Tom Kerridge joins the judges in Angela Hartnett's place. Then it's down to business as Mary Berry asks them to cook their ultimate family pie and Chris Bavin challenges them to rustle up a dish using a root vegetable usually served on the side. As usual, those who least impress the judges compete in the eliminator, a recipe close to Tom's heart that he expects to be executed perfectly. Presented by Claudia Winkleman

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Short
programme.desc: The food enthusiasts cook traditional pasties at Geevor Tin Mine in Cornwall, then head northwards to Melton Mowbray, where they try their hand at making pork pies. The duo's trip ends on the banks of the River Deveron in Scotland, where they cook salmon coulibiac - a whole fish stuffed with prawns wrapped in filo pastry - for Highland dancers and pipers

programme.title: Blue Planet UK
programme.desc: Steve Brown, Gillian Burke and Chris Packham investigate the health of the UK's waters and look at stories featuring marine wildlife. Steve reveals how ocean plastic rubbish is turned into kayaks used to help clean up the seas, Gillian goes oyster fishing in Cornwall and Chris meets author Philip Hoare, who thinks everyone should take a daily dip in the sea

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Michael Baggott assess items brought in to Grittleton House in Wiltshire, while presenter Paul Martin visits Fairfield House on the outskirts of Bath

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Previous winners Hugh Dennis, Sarah Greene, Nish Kumar and Holly Walsh go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo takes a trip in a Second World War Spitfire above Biggin Hill aerodrome, and takes the train to East Grinstead on the trail of Winnie the Pooh. He also visits Plaw Hatch Farm, 200 acres of community-owned farmland that operates biodynamically according to principles first laid out in the 1920s

programme.title: Nigellissima
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson demonstrates how to prepare a range of straightforward yet sumptuous Italian dishes, including her recipe for roast chicken with saffron orzotto. She also creates two desserts - coffee ice-cream and a chocolate olive-oil cake - and a pasta dish with courgettes that is ideal for late-night snacking

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox is joined by comedian Rachel Parris, chef Rick Stein, actor and comedian Rob Delaney and presenter Vick Hope. They share favourite books as well as reviewing the week's two club picks - The Lying Life of Adults by Elena Ferrante, and Should We Fall Behind by Sharon Duggal

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Andi Oliver presents as the eight winning chefs from the regional heats battle to get their starter course to the banquet celebrating British invention and innovation. The rivals are cooking to impress Matthew Fort, Oliver Peyton, Rachel Khoo and guest judge comedian Phil Wang

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: The mums and dad join the merry-go-round of secondary school open days as they face the choice of where to send their children, with Julia panicking about which catchment area she lives in and even considering a fake religious conversion to beat the system. As Kevin's divorce progresses, Liz helps him navigate the shark-infested world of lawyers, and makes a worrying discovery about her own situation along the way, while Amanda struggles with playing second fiddle to Meg's illness

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Everyone says the season seven finale of fantasy epic The Ninth Circle was pretty disappointing - even show-runner Spencer Maguire is keen to move on to new projects. But not everyone thinks that it has to be the end of the matter, and obsessive fan Simon Smethurst decides to visit his hero to help put things right. Written by and starring Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with Nick Mohammed and Lindsay Duncan

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Jo Brand and poet Lemn Sissay joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: The First Team
programme.desc: The players are given a presentation on the rules of sexual conduct and consent, after which Mattie, Benji and Jack head out for the afternoon determined to keep out of trouble. However, a chance supermarket meeting with a woman who wants them to meet her son Stuart leads to an unfortunate misunderstanding. As Stuart threatens to go public with his side of the story, Mattie and the others try to placate him with an all-access visit to the training complex to meet the team. Starring Shaquille Ali-Yebuah, Jack McMullen, Jake Short and Theo Barklem Biggs

programme.title: The First Team
programme.desc: With the rest of the team away on international duties, Petey announces he is hosting a barbecue. But when Mattie, Benji and Jack find they are the only guests, a long, strange afternoon unfolds. Meanwhile, Jack's dad oversteps the mark by posting club business on social media and Cesare's job looks uncertain. Have the fans turned against him? Comedy, starring Starring Shaquille Ali-Yebuah, Jack McMullen, Jake Short and Theo Barklem-Biggs

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison explore Cranborne Chase, help tend to Britain's biggest modern-day maze, and meet farmers who are working together to encourage wildlife on their land. Steve Brown goes stargazing, Tom Heap reports on the possible environmental costs of a new space race launched from rural sites, and Adam Henson takes his rare breed Suffolk Punch to the maternity unit

programme.title: A Life in Ten Pictures
programme.desc: Ten photographs that reveal more about the defining moments in the boxer's remarkable life and career, featuring interviews with those who knew him best - including his daughter Jamillah Ali, journalist Gary Younge and Olympic swimmer Janet Evans. The film follows a young Cassius Clay as he 'shook up the world' in more ways than one, before his controversial name change and membership of the Nation of Islam, all the way through to the lighting of the Olympic flame in Atlanta in 1996

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The Roadshow is at Stonor Park in Oxfordshire, where finds include a rare group of Indian medals, Florence Nightingale's toolkit and the story of a remarkable female pilot from the Second World War. There is also an intriguing painting by an unknown artist, some computer generated art with a surprisingly high value and a collection of rare pearls from around the world

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is at Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey with experts Nell Wragg and Sarah Peterson, who restore an unwanted windsurf sail, a chest of drawers, and a child's cot

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Newbie Irita Marriott brings Latvian flair to the game as she takes on Tim Medhurst in a sporting tour of the antiques emporia of Lincolnshire. One expert has a brush with the law

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: In Adam Frost's garden, he gives his tips on lawn repair, grows some unusual vegetables and plants a seasonal container. Meanwhile, Advolly Richmond brings us up to date in her history of lawns

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc, as pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords, in an attempt to take home £2,500 in cash

programme.title: Good Morning Dagenham
programme.desc: To celebrate the depth of talent among the traders and customers, the team puts on a talent show, while a new trader arrives at the market with a surprising idea for making money

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Matt's insistence on finding the mother who gave Georgie up for adoption has unforeseen consequences for himself and Olivia. When top surgeon Neil Burton arrives at Inverness Hospital it becomes immediately obvious that he is not there for Henry's job. Anna has a shock for Mrs Collins

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman presents the semi-final, which sees the four remaining cooks produce a selection of party canapes and rustle up a dish from a tinned store cupboard staple. The ones who impress the least must fight for their place in the final by cooking again in the eliminator - and this week, Angela Hartnett gets them to produce a recipe also happens to be her husband's favourite dessert. Who will be hanging up their apron at the last hurdle?

programme.title: Blue Planet UK
programme.desc: Steve Brown uncovers the historic secrets of seabirds in Yorkshire, Gillian Burke finds out sea creatures become stressed, and Chris Packham meets a couple adding to shark science

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Nick Davies scour the crowd at Tilbury Docks in Essex to find objects to take off to auction, while Paul Martin explores the history of the docks

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Hugh Dennis, Sarah Greene, Nish Kumar and Holly Walsh go head to head testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Hassocks and makes his way to the beautiful Sussex village of Ditchling, where a Roman Catholic community of artists once made their home. Next, he visits the seaside town of Bexhill-on-Sea, before the Kent and East Sussex heritage railway conveys Michael from Bodiam Castle through the scenic Rother Valley to Tenterden, and finally arrives in the village of Benenden

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The eight regional champions cook their fish courses as they battle to win a place on the menu of a banquet celebrating British innovation. Engineer and TV presenter Zoe Laughlin joins the judging panel of food critic Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and food creative Rachel Khoo, to decide who should make it to the banquet

programme.title: All That Glitters: Britain's Next Jewellery Star
programme.desc: It's the grand final, and the three finalists return to the workshop to compete to be crowned the winner. They are each challenged to make two pieces of truly outstanding jewellery - pair of pearl earrings and a bridal headpiece. Who will the judges decide is worthy of the title Britain's next big jewellery star?

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: Documentary following the day-to-day realities the NHS faces in unprecedented time, focusing on the issues and challenges that continually dominate the headlines. University Hospital Coventry's gynaecological oncology department deals with rising referrals, a growing backlog and complex cases as the hospital's theatre capacity attempts to return to normal

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Guests Lucy Porter, Frankie Boyle and Ross Noble join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'next' in this extended episode. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Mark Lawson Talks To
programme.desc: The journalist interviews the renowned actress about her life and career, including her award-winning performances in TV series Little Dorrit, the film Age of Innocence, and more recent appearance as Professor Sprout in the Harry Potter movies

programme.title: Saved By a Stranger
programme.desc: Cameras focus on Navy veteran David, who survived an attack on the SS Atlantic Conveyor in May 1982, during the Falklands war. David is desperate to reconnect with his fellow crewmen. Plus, the story of Darryl, an author whose family came to the UK as part of the post-war wave of migration from former British colonies. He searches for the teachers who helped him overcome childhood racism and xenophobia. Anita Rani presents

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Documentary highlighting how British Jews from across the faith celebrate life's big milestones, from birth to coming of age, marriage to the end of life. A 12-year-old prepares for his bar mitzvah, a couple marry in Spain, a modern ceremony they have adapted according to their beliefs, and at Bushey Cemetery in Hertfordshire, a woman describes her role as a volunteer who does the tahara, the ritual washing of the deceased before burial

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: A look at how British Hindus mark life's big milestones. In south London, Bhumi and Chirag are marking the birth of their six-day-old son with a traditional ceremony at home, inviting the gods to write his future. Meanwhile, when 18-year-old Samahit leaves for university, his family travel with him to bless his new home at the halls of residence

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick heads into the county's china clay pits to discover what is known locally as white gold, one of its most important industries. He then climbs the Cornish Alps, huge slag heaps created by the clay mining and some of Cornwall's highest land. After a wild swim at one of Cornwall's picturesque hidden coves, Rick cooks a simple sea bass recipe, revealing how to make the perfect mayonnaise. In the far west of the county, on the Land's End peninsula, Rick meets his niece, a modern artist, who explains her deep spiritual connection to Cornwall

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Revolution and Romance: Musical Masters of the 19th Century
programme.desc: Suzy Klein's odyssey through the history of music in the 19th century concludes, turning the spotlight on later years, when technological revolutions seemed to make the world a smaller, more accessible place. Focusing on the Paris Exposition of 1889, a cultural showcase celebrating the centenary of the French Revoution, she reveals how composers such as Claude Debussy were exposed to the sounds of the East - immediately breaking Wagner's stranglehold of influence over Western music. She also examines the invention of saxophone, and makes her own recording on a vintage phonograph - an early precursor of vinyl records, cassette tapes and CDs

programme.title: Sound Waves: The Symphony of Physics
programme.desc: Helen Czerski investigates the science behind familiar sounds as well as those humans cannot hear. At the Palace of Westminster, she teams up with scientists to carry out measurements using state-of-the-art lasers to reveal how Big Ben vibrates to create pressure waves in the air at particular frequencies. She also explores the science of the voice, inviting soprano Lesley Garrett to sing I Dreamed A Dream inside an MRI scanner to reveal how her vocal tract acts as a resonator, amplifying and shaping the sound from her larynx

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: Docudrama portrait of Delia Derbyshire, the electronic sound pioneer behind the Doctor Who theme tune, exploring the idea that this extraordinary composer herself lived outside of time and space. Delia's story is told through two archives: the first, a collection of lost works, 267 reels of quarter-inch magnetic tape recordings of Delia's work found in her attic after her death, the other, her school books, paintings and keepsakes, discovered in her childhood bedroom

programme.title: BBC Proms 2018
programme.desc: From London's Royal Albert Hall, ground-breaking British composer Anna Meredith presents this tribute to the godmothers of electronica, the pioneers of the BBC Radiophonic Workshop. The celebration begins with music by Delia Derbyshire - most famously remembered for bringing the world the Doctor Who theme in its full electronic glory and finishes with the premiere of Daphne Oram's Still Point, pieced together from recently discovered archive material and performed by Shiva Feshareki on turntables. Music by artists including Laurie Spiegel, Chaines and Suzanne Ciani emphasise the power of this legacy, performed by the London Contemporary Orchestra under conductor Robert Ames.

programme.title: Dawn Chorus: The Sounds of Spring
programme.desc: Filmed without voiceover or music, this programme highlights the call of the birds at sunrise, as those living within Britain's habitats burst into song and reveal the natural splendour of their distinctive chorus

programme.title: Into the Wind
programme.desc: Writer and birdwatcher Tim Dee walks along the vast open flatlands of the Lincolnshire Wash, on a quest to capture the elusive sound of 'pure' wind. On his travels, he reflects on the landscape, walking, writing, birds and wind. From documentary film maker Richard Alwyn

programme.title: Sound Waves: The Symphony of Physics
programme.desc: Helen Czerski investigates the science behind familiar sounds as well as those humans cannot hear. At the Palace of Westminster, she teams up with scientists to carry out measurements using state-of-the-art lasers to reveal how Big Ben vibrates to create pressure waves in the air at particular frequencies. She also explores the science of the voice, inviting soprano Lesley Garrett to sing I Dreamed A Dream inside an MRI scanner to reveal how her vocal tract acts as a resonator, amplifying and shaping the sound from her larynx

programme.title: Revolution and Romance: Musical Masters of the 19th Century
programme.desc: Suzy Klein's odyssey through the history of music in the 19th century concludes, turning the spotlight on later years, when technological revolutions seemed to make the world a smaller, more accessible place. Focusing on the Paris Exposition of 1889, a cultural showcase celebrating the centenary of the French Revoution, she reveals how composers such as Claude Debussy were exposed to the sounds of the East - immediately breaking Wagner's stranglehold of influence over Western music. She also examines the invention of saxophone, and makes her own recording on a vintage phonograph - an early precursor of vinyl records, cassette tapes and CDs

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming explores the English canal network, beginning with a journey from Shardlow to Etruria Junction, Stoke on Trent, exploring the Trent and Mersey Canal. However, not everything goes to plan, and he will have to deal with a tricky issue with his propeller as well as navigating the complicated locks on the Trent and Mersey Canal

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob travels back to days of swimming in a lovely secluded bend of a river, and brings back memories of a forgotten time

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: The team examines an 18th-century portrait attributed to pioneering female artist Maria Cosway, but which Philip Mould believes may actually be an undiscovered work by the great Regency artist Sir Thomas Lawrence. He noticed it during a visit to the owners' home and now the time has come to put his hunch to the test. What's more, the difference in value is enormous - as a work by Cosway, it is worth about £8,000. If the presenter is right, it could be more in the region of half a million

programme.title: Classic British Cars: Made in Coventry
programme.desc: To celebrate the award of UK City of Culture 2021 to Coventry, local boy Mark Evans tells the full history of the classic cars made in Coventry. This passionate retrospective runs from the industry's beginnings in 1897 through to the present day and includes interviews and archive footage detailing the stories behind the creation of classic marques such as the Daimler, the Triumph, the Riley and the Jaguar and the companies and people behind them

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob talks to the poet and playwright, who recently published his memoir My Name Is Why. He discusses what it was like growing up as the only black child in a sleepy market town outside Wigan in the 1970s, his time in care and how he was separated from his foster family at the age of 12 and left to fend for himself. He also returns to Ethiopia to find out more about his roots. Featuring contributions from colleagues including Steve Coogan, Benjamin Zephaniah, Linton Kwesi Johnson and Julie Hesmondhalgh

programme.title: Rhymes, Rock and Revolution - The Story of Performance Poetry
programme.desc: Lauren Laverne narrates a documentary exploring how the lines between popular music and poetry have become blurred in the years since beat poet Allen Ginsberg's 1965 appearance at the Royal Albert Hall, which captured the imagination of young people aligned to the emergent counter-culture movement. They also reveal how this revolution in verse continues today, in the form of thriving urban music and slam poetry scenes. With contributions by John Cooper Clarke, Hollie McNish and Phill Jupitus

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob travels back to days of swimming in a lovely secluded bend of a river, and brings back memories of a forgotten time

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming explores the English canal network, beginning with a journey from Shardlow to Etruria Junction, Stoke on Trent, exploring the Trent and Mersey Canal. programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming explores the English canal network. A low canal tunnel in Staffordshire proves to be problematic, and Robbie helps out a stranded boater

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross has more painting tips as he captures on canvas a lone cabin nestled in a snow-covered landscape

programme.title: Eurovision Song Contest 2021
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal, Scott Mills and Chelcee Grimes commentate on the opening semi-final from Rotterdam, which sees the first 16 countries competing for a place in Saturday's grand final. The show is opened by the Netherlands' Duncan Laurence, the most recent winner from 2019, before the hopeful nations - Lithuania, Slovenia, Russia, Sweden, Australia, North Macedonia, Ireland, Cyprus, Norway, Croatia, Belgium, Israel, Romania, Azerbaijan, Ukraine and Malta - take to the stage

programme.title: Extra Life: A Short History of Living Longer
programme.desc: Science writer Steven Johnson and historian David Olusoga embark on an investigative journey through 300 years of scientific breakthroughs and go behind the scenes of modern medicine to meet the unsung heroes tackling Covid-19 and other public health threats in today's world. They begin by examining how the development of vaccines has helped to prevent the spread of serious disease and how new ones are developed when never-before-seen diseases emerge

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: The vitamin industry is worth more than £400million a year in the UK alone, but what are these pills actually doing for people's health? Dr Giles Yeo aims to find out, exploring if vitamin and mineral supplements are what they appear to be and putting people's diets to the test to find out if they really need these supplements. Those he meets include a scientist in Copenhagen, whose groundbreaking research into antioxidants indicates they may not have the extraordinary health-enhancing properties that people believe, and a nutrition scientist from the University of Greenwich, who tells Giles that on average most people in the UK are meeting their vitamin requirements thorough their diet

programme.title: Pain, Pus & Poison: The Search for Modern Medicines
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how scientists exploited the natural world to make medicines, beginning by investigating the challenges they faced trying to create a drug that would alleviate pain. The programme examines what physical discomfort actually is, why people want to control it, and how the discovery of morphine led to 200 years of scientific breakthroughs and self-experimentation

programme.title: The Beauty of Anatomy
programme.desc: First in a five-part series in which Dr Adam Rutherford investigates the close relationship between discoveries in anatomy and the works of art that illustrate them. He begins by focusing on Roman anatomist Claudius Galen and artist and part-time dissector Leonardo da Vinci

programme.desc: Family comedy adapted from David Walliams' children's book about 12-year-old Dennis, an ordinary boy who lives in an ordinary town - but somehow feels different from everyone else. But can a boy really wear a dress - and what will his headmaster, dad and friends in the football team think if they find out? programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: Temperatures rise as the zoo is hit by a heatwave. Many of the animals are well adapted to the sun, but those from cooler climates need extra attention. The boys devise a series of ingenious tricks to keep each animal cool, including ice baths, mist showers and giant ice lollies. It’s fish flavour for the penguins, lettuce lollies for Marion the tortoise and a huge blood lolly for the big cats. Meanwhile, the camels get their yearly comb-down, Tyler has to muck out the stinky Penguin pool and the boys play with an orphaned elephant in Uganda.

programme.title: Maddie, the Plants and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie on an adventure around the world of trees to see how we can help look after them and our planet. Discover how trees grow, how we can tell the age of trees and how the roots, leaves and bark work. Maddie shows how trees are home to lots of other plants, animals, birds and insects who live on and amongst them and have an important role in our ecosystem. We’ll discover a tree’s leaves make energy using sunlight, so they can keep growing. We learn that a tree is alive, it drinks and breathes and grows just like we do! And we’ll find out about deciduous trees that lose their leaves in winter and evergreens that keep their leaves all year round.

programme.title: Bluey

programme.title: Raa Raa the Noisy Lion

programme.title: Yakka Dee!

We see a red toothbrush, a blue toothbrush, a new toothbrush, a fuzzy toothbrush and even a giant toothbrush! Dee and a friend have fun brushing their teeth, and there’s even a toothbrush in a rush! The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee’s friend finally succeeds in saying today’s word!

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Pug wants to break a world record. Dog suggests they go to the North Pole so that Pug can be the first pug to reach the Pole. They have some help from a Walrus, but when they arrive they see Granny Pug has got there first. Pug breaks another world record.

programme.title: Bing

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friend play pizza restaurants, and Mr Tumble knits a jumper

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa and Po have a good time rolling a ball down a hill and then rolling each other down it. Then, the Teletubbies enjoy watching children have fun rolling down the slopes too

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Reau, Alfie, Charlie, Ollie, Otis, River and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag is a night light, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that could be used as part of a bedtime routine. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate bedtime objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The baby lullaby Little One Time For Bed features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show what they’ve been doing at bedtime too. The Baby Bear Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Energetic animation for children aged two to five, following Duggee the dog and his Squirrel Club

programme.title: My Petsaurus
programme.desc: Fluffy's toilet training doesn't quite go as well as Megan had hoped

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: The Flier Upper Muncher loves eating meteorites. The floating rocks are home to the Grouchy Wrigglers and they are not happy with their home being eaten! B.O.T. has to balance on the Muncher’s head and avoid being munched to complete the mission.

programme.title: Garden Tales
programme.desc: Annie and Alex are two young swans trying to work out what they want to be when they grow up. Alex wants to be a good singer, just like Jago the Robin with his beautiful song. Annie wants to be on the catwalk like Simon the Stickleback, who has a vibrant sense of colour. But they’re not sure they’ll ever be able to do either of those things. Will they ever discover what they want to be?

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk to a place where they can make musical instruments using sticks. On the way they gallop like knights on horseback at a castle, play some music in the park, walk through a tunnel that feels like a cave, and even blast off into space!

programme.title: Magic Hands

programme.title: Timmy Time

programme.title: Tik Tak
programme.desc: Explore a construction worker made of beads and watch as a cheeky chameleon plays with a bee. Meanwhile, Book Girl makes a wheelbarrow.

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join Stevie and Conor as they begin to discover the world of sound and see how their hearing is developed. Conor’s big brother Harrison is here to help show him around all his different musical toys, including a noisy rattle. In the garden, Stevie hangs out with mum and dad and listens to all the different sounds before getting ready to go out. Meanwhile, Conor heads over to grandma’s house while listening for sounds with his mum and brother. Stevie gets a quick hearing test at the hospital before heading home to make lots of noise with household objects. After making rainmakers at grandma’s house, Harrison and Conor get hold of a set of walkie talkies to play with. When the day is almost finished, it’s time for Stevie's parents to sing her a lullaby to help send her to sleep.

programme.title: Woolly & Tig

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Norbet is learning to play Squeezy-Box, but Deeno cannot concentrate

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Eddie, whose mum is Italian, makes an authentic Italian cheese and olive pizza for his friends, using home-made dough and cooking it outside in his family's pizza oven

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: It's the hottest day of the year and Waffle is eating ice cream to cool down. Outside in the sweltering heat, Simon turns on the sprinkler to help everyone cool down, but poor Waffle doesn't like the sprinkler and darts through the hole in the fence to Mrs Hobbs's garden, looking for shade. The Brooklyn-Bells try to coax him back, but he refuses to return. Doug decides to make a mini swimming pool by filling the ball pit with cool water. Waffle loves swimming pools. Maybe that will encourage him back?

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘toothbrush’, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see a red toothbrush, a blue toothbrush, a new toothbrush, a fuzzy toothbrush and even a giant toothbrush! Dee and a friend have fun brushing their teeth, and there’s even a toothbrush in a rush! The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee’s friend finally succeeds in saying today’s word!

programme.desc: Dog finds the original book that starts all of his adventures. He shows Pug how he started the bookshop and how he read all the books, so he could recommend which books each customer should read. It was a great success but Pug was not in this story.

programme.desc: Maddie Moate visits a fairground and goes for a ride on a carousel using a special camera to find out how it works. Next, Maddie visits a factory to see how different coloured wax crayons are made from paraffin oil and how a big mold creates hundreds of crayons in one go

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Grandmaster Glitch's digging floods Wieliczka Saltmine. The Go Jetters must save the famous salt statues

programme.title: Moon and Me

programme.title: Clangers
