programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Frank Skinner reads Russell the Sheep, by Roc Scotton

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Southampton v Fulham at St Mary's Stadium and Brighton & Hove Albion v West Ham United at Amex Stadium. Fulham and Brighton are still battling it out to avoid relegation to the Championship, while the Hammers were looking to move a step closer to securing a place in Europe. Plus, Burnley v Leeds United and the Friday night contest between Newcastle United and Manchester City

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Leicester City. Gabby Logan presents action from the final at Wembley Stadium, as the sides battled it out for the prestigious trophy. The Blues secured their place in the showpiece match with a 1-0 last-four victory over Manchester City, while Leicester overcame Southampton by the same scoreline

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: Andrew's guests are Matt Hancock MP, health secretary, and Secretary John Kerry, US special envoy on climate. With news review from Simon Jack, BBC business editor, and Kate Garraway, presenter of ITV's Good Morning Britain.

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around Wales. Presented by James Williams

programme.title: Sunday Morning Stories
programme.desc: Rev Kate Bottley hears from people who have given up their time to help others, including the UK's youngest chief executive and a couple who offered their home to a Syrian refugee

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Santiago
programme.desc: The seven celebrities continue their trek along the Camino de Santiago pilgrimage to their destination of the Cathedral of Santiago de Compostela. The Rev Kate Bottley discovers a fountain of free-flowing wine, while Ed Byrne meets a man who believes money is the root of all evil. As the group bonds strengthen, JJ Chalmers and Neil Morrissey open up about traumatic experiences that have shaped their lives, while Debbie McGee leaves a stone bearing the name of her late husband, Paul Daniels, at the foot of the cathedral's famous Iron Cross. Narrated by Lee Ingleby

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Raj Bisram help two teams at Southwell racecourse in Nottinghamshire make a profit at auction. Hosted by Anita Manning

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones explores the Cathedral town of Exeter and, in Dementia Action Week, YolanDa Brown meets one local woman helping people with dementia to remain active

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers have their say on the BBC's output and there are behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple in the Welsh county of Ceredigion, looking to buy a country home with the potential for a spiritual retreat on a budget of £355,000

programme.title: Cars 3 (2017)
programme.desc: Constantly outshined and outraced by a fleet of hi-tech rookies, former champ Lightning McQueen must find a new way to win. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Owen Wilson, Cristela Alonzo and Chris Cooper

programme.title: Iolo: The Last Wilderness of Wales
programme.desc: Iolo Williams tracks wildlife in the Cambrian mountains, the remote uplands that form the heart and spine of Wale. His first target is the elusive pine marten, finding one at night lurking in a conifer forest, and Iolo also looks for ospreys fishing on Llyn Clywedog

programme.title: Weatherman Walking: The Welsh Coast
programme.desc: Derek Brockway takes the circular route around the Great Orme on a trek from Deganwy to Llandudno, and as part of his journey braves his nerves alongside the elements to abseil down a section of the massive rock. Mo Jannah tries his hand at traditional mussel fishing, while Erin Lloyd-Jones explores the history of Llandudno Pier, meeting the designer's great, great grandson

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith head to the Peak District to celebrate the 70th anniversary of the UK's first National Park. Matt joins a pioneering scheme to restore ruined farm buildings and meets 'legend' of the Peak District Gordon Miller, one of the area's early rangers and a last link with those who brought about the National Park. Charlotte channels her inner artist to help to create a giant anniversary art mural and records the special sounds of the Park's waterways, while Joe Crowley investigates new pollution laws causing controversy in the countryside

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The Roadshow is at Stonor Park in Oxfordshire, where finds include a rare group of Indian medals, Florence Nightingale's toolkit and the story of a remarkable female pilot from the Second World War. There is also an intriguing painting by an unknown artist, some computer generated art with a surprisingly high value and a collection of rare pearls from around the world

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne is concerned when a toddler displays troubling symptoms, while an overly protective mother struggles to cope with her daughter's teenage pregnancy and is insistent on keeping it low key, much to the frustration of new Pupil Midwife Nancy

programme.title: The Pursuit of Love
programme.desc: Fanny embraces motherhood and tries to live up to society's image of a good wife, while Linda throws herself into a lifestyle of all-night parties as her marriage to Tony almost immediately falls apart. When she is pulled away by an enigmatic stranger, she sees the chance to make a drastic change and start a whole new life

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including West Bromwich Albion v Liverpool at The Hawthorns. Sam Allardyce's men held the Reds to a 1-1 draw when the sides met at Anfield in December in what was the start of a downturn in form for Liverpool. Plus, Everton v Sheffield United, Crystal Palace v Aston Villa and Tottenham Hotspur v Wolverhampton Wanderers

programme.title: Once Were Lions
programme.desc: Documentary following the rugby league Great Britain Lions' tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea, as they fight for their sporting future

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Angela Rippon and Julia Somerville are back in the studio, investigating the latest scams doing the rounds and finding out how consumers can get a good deal while they are out and about

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Developers with a pressing deadline of their own race to refurbish a terraced house in Tylorstown in south Wales, a businesswoman buys a former takeaway to help her business, and a developer got more than he bargains for when he buys a piece of land on the Isle of Sheppey. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow progress as the auction buys are developed

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: A web of deceit from a greedy council tenant, and police foil a criminal gang running a sophisticated £8 million fake transport ticket fraud

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A farm engineer's new truck loses a wheel while he's driving home, and a five-year-old boy uses the number on his toy ambulance to call for help when his mum is taken ill

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Ochucko Ojiri help the teams to find bargains in Leominster, Herefordshire, and Christina Trevanion learns about oil painting restoration

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie gets some dating advice on her first day of full training as a healthcare assistant with Luca. Rob and Harriet attend an unusual crime scene, where a woman is found tied up in the boot of her own car

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Maureen, Matt, Marisa and Stefan vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple from Scotland look for a property in the Cotswolds on a budget of £600,000, and drops in on show jumping double Olympic gold medallist Nick Skelton

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is at Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey with experts Nell Wragg and Sarah Peterson, who restore an unwanted windsurf sail, a chest of drawers, and a child's cot

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Newbie Irita Marriott brings Latvian flair to the game as she takes on Tim Medhurst in a sporting tour of the antiques emporia of Lincolnshire. One expert has a brush with the law

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: The team goes undercover to catch a fly-tipper who's been dumping rubbish on the streets, and Mo finds out just how powerful the cameras are on our phones

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Molly is taken aback to learn about Bernie's surrogacy plan and asks her not to go through with it, for the sake of their relationship. Denise bumps into a familiar face down at the allotments, and Sharon tells Zack to stop bringing women back to the flat. Nancy encourages Mick to finish his victim statement and read it in court himself - but unknown to them, Katy is back in the Square

programme.title: BBC Wales Investigates
programme.desc: A former Wales rugby international takes on his biggest challenge - to prove the game he loves has caused his dementia. As he and a group of retired players prepare to take their fight against rugby's governing bodies to court, Wyre Davies investigates whether opportunities to protect players from brain injuries have been missed, and asks whether the game is about to be changed forever

programme.title: The Pact
programme.desc: When a young brewery boss is found dead, a chain of events is triggered that draws four of his employees into a fragile pact of silence that will change their lives for ever. Anna and her friends Nancy, Louie and Cat work at the brewery under young boss Jack Evans, whose bullying, spite and manipulation has taken its toll. After too many drinks at a work function, the women make a snap decision to bundle Jack into a car and dump him in the woods as a prank - but soon after, Jack is found dead. Starring Laura Fraser, Eiry Thomas, Heledd Gwynn and Julie Hesmondhalgh

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: Starstruck
programme.desc: Kate receives an invitation to Tom's premiere and forces a reluctant Jessie to tag along. Tom and Jessie are interrupted by an unexpected visitor

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Morgan is desperate to know if ex Josh wanted an open relationship. With the truth finally out could they have a future together? Tasheeka thought she'd found her perfect match in Dexter until he suddenly ghosted her. He's ready to reveal the truth but will it be too much to swallow? Childhood sweethearts Daniel and Scarlett were plain sailing until he left her to go travelling. Could there be any way back for these lovebirds?

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race Down Under
programme.desc: The Queens are split in two, as they compete in the ultimate Girl Group sing-off, as the quest to become Down Under's first Drag Race Superstar continues

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Heather Small, as they assist Caitlin and Kristen from Warrington in picking out the best singers from a line-up that includes a singing wrestler, a model and a dentist

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Fawlty Towers

programme.title: The Pact
programme.desc: As the police begin interviewing the brewery workers about Jack's background and his behaviour at the party, the women go to extraordinary lengths to protect their dark secret. However, someone else knows what happened that night and is now holding them to ransom. Drama, starring Laura Fraser, Julie Hesmondhalgh, Eiry Thomas and Heledd Gwynn, with Eddie Marsan and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the opening matches in the penultimate round of Premier League fixtures, including Chelsea v Leicester City at Stamford Bridge. Chelsea and the Foxes were facing each other for the second time in four days, having also met in the FA Cup final, and this contest could have proved to be just as important as the clubs jostled for positions inside the top four. Plus, Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester City, Manchester United v Fulham and Southampton v Leeds United

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The amateur make-up artists are booked for a photo shoot for online retailer ASOS. Back in the studio, the amateurs create a look that is 'out of this world', before the two weakest face off in the elimination. Presented by Maya Jama

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norma turns to Romero for guidance when she finds herself in an unexpected position of power. As the pair push the limits with the powerful Bob Paris, Norman and Dylan both come undone at the fallout of a secret, while Caleb considers an offer made to him by a surprising source

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Emma grows closer to Dylan when they both go to Norman's aid. Meanwhile, Norma begins to consider a life away from White Pine Bay

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Southampton v Fulham at St Mary's Stadium and Brighton & Hove Albion v West Ham United at Amex Stadium. Fulham and Brighton are still battling it out to avoid relegation to the Championship, while the Hammers were looking to move a step closer to securing a place in Europe. Plus, Burnley v Leeds United and the Friday night contest between Newcastle United and Manchester City

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Leicester City. Gabby Logan presents action from the final at Wembley Stadium, as the sides battled it out for the prestigious trophy. The Blues secured their place in the showpiece match with a 1-0 last-four victory over Manchester City, while Leicester overcame Southampton by the same scoreline

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: Andrew's guests are Matt Hancock MP, health secretary, and Secretary John Kerry, US special envoy on climate. With news review from Simon Jack, BBC business editor, and Kate Garraway, presenter of ITV's Good Morning Britain.

programme.title: The Sunday Show
programme.desc: Martin Geissler presents the main issues from the world of politics and beyond, featuring interviews, discussion and analysis on the stories that matter to Scotland

programme.title: Sunday Morning Stories
programme.desc: Rev Kate Bottley hears from people who have given up their time to help others, including the UK's youngest chief executive and a couple who offered their home to a Syrian refugee

programme.title: Reflections at the Quay
programme.desc: Rev Mandy Ralph of Annbank Parish Church, Ayr, and Fr David Wallace, Our Lady of Lourdes, Cardonald, lead todays Reflections on the theme of Unity. Music is by Celtic Worship

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich help a Bedfordshire woman pay tribute to her late husband. Maureen wants her new garden to be a reminder of her and her Graham's life together, with a sense of their old mountain views mixed with a Moroccan-cum-Cordoba style courtyard, inspired by their travels. Can Charlie and the Rich brothers transport this blank canvas to Spain on a £3,000 budget?

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Raj Bisram help two teams at Southwell racecourse in Nottinghamshire make a profit at auction. Hosted by Anita Manning

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones explores the Cathedral town of Exeter and, in Dementia Action Week, YolanDa Brown meets one local woman helping people with dementia to remain active

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers have their say on the BBC's output and there are behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple in the Welsh county of Ceredigion, looking to buy a country home with the potential for a spiritual retreat on a budget of £355,000

programme.title: Cars 3 (2017)
programme.desc: Constantly outshined and outraced by a fleet of hi-tech rookies, former champ Lightning McQueen must find a new way to win. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Owen Wilson, Cristela Alonzo and Chris Cooper

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates footage of a lion pride on Kenya's Masai Mara that has reached a dangerous and unusual turning point where the future of the group hangs in the balance. The adult males that had defended the pride have all deserted the group, leaving two remaining adult females to protect their eight cubs from a wide range of threats, including rival prides, herds of buffalo and the constant menace of marauding hyenas. However, the greatest threat of all comes when the lions encounter humans

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith head to the Peak District to celebrate the 70th anniversary of the UK's first National Park. Matt joins a pioneering scheme to restore ruined farm buildings and meets 'legend' of the Peak District Gordon Miller, one of the area's early rangers and a last link with those who brought about the National Park. Charlotte channels her inner artist to help to create a giant anniversary art mural and records the special sounds of the Park's waterways, while Joe Crowley investigates new pollution laws causing controversy in the countryside

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The Roadshow is at Stonor Park in Oxfordshire, where finds include a rare group of Indian medals, Florence Nightingale's toolkit and the story of a remarkable female pilot from the Second World War. There is also an intriguing painting by an unknown artist, some computer generated art with a surprisingly high value and a collection of rare pearls from around the world

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne is concerned when a toddler displays troubling symptoms, while an overly protective mother struggles to cope with her daughter's teenage pregnancy and is insistent on keeping it low key, much to the frustration of new Pupil Midwife Nancy

programme.title: The Pursuit of Love
programme.desc: Fanny embraces motherhood and tries to live up to society's image of a good wife, while Linda throws herself into a lifestyle of all-night parties as her marriage to Tony almost immediately falls apart. When she is pulled away by an enigmatic stranger, she sees the chance to make a drastic change and start a whole new life

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including West Bromwich Albion v Liverpool at The Hawthorns. Sam Allardyce's men held the Reds to a 1-1 draw when the sides met at Anfield in December in what was the start of a downturn in form for Liverpool. Plus, Everton v Sheffield United, Crystal Palace v Aston Villa and Tottenham Hotspur v Wolverhampton Wanderers

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Action from the final Premiership fixtures of the season, featuring Hamilton Academical v Kilmarnock at Fountain of Youth Stadium, Motherwell v Ross County at Fir Park and St Mirren v Dundee United at Simple Digital Arena

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Angela Rippon and Julia Somerville are back in the studio, investigating the latest scams doing the rounds and finding out how consumers can get a good deal while they are out and about

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Developers with a pressing deadline of their own race to refurbish a terraced house in Tylorstown in south Wales, a businesswoman buys a former takeaway to help her business, and a developer got more than he bargains for when he buys a piece of land on the Isle of Sheppey. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow progress as the auction buys are developed

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: A web of deceit from a greedy council tenant, and police foil a criminal gang running a sophisticated £8 million fake transport ticket fraud

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A farm engineer's new truck loses a wheel while he's driving home, and a five-year-old boy uses the number on his toy ambulance to call for help when his mum is taken ill

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Ochucko Ojiri help the teams to find bargains in Leominster, Herefordshire, and Christina Trevanion learns about oil painting restoration

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie gets some dating advice on her first day of full training as a healthcare assistant with Luca. Rob and Harriet attend an unusual crime scene, where a woman is found tied up in the boot of her own car

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Maureen, Matt, Marisa and Stefan vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple from Scotland look for a property in the Cotswolds on a budget of £600,000, and drops in on show jumping double Olympic gold medallist Nick Skelton

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is at Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey with experts Nell Wragg and Sarah Peterson, who restore an unwanted windsurf sail, a chest of drawers, and a child's cot

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Newbie Irita Marriott brings Latvian flair to the game as she takes on Tim Medhurst in a sporting tour of the antiques emporia of Lincolnshire. One expert has a brush with the law

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: Scotland's Home of the Year
programme.desc: The judges travel to Orkney and Shetland as Anna Campbell-Jones, Michael Angus and Kate Spiers continue their search for Scotland's Home of the Year. Properties include a listed terrace home in Orkney and a renovated croft and a new build in Shetland

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Molly is taken aback to learn about Bernie's surrogacy plan and asks her not to go through with it, for the sake of their relationship. Denise bumps into a familiar face down at the allotments, and Sharon tells Zack to stop bringing women back to the flat. Nancy encourages Mick to finish his victim statement and read it in court himself - but unknown to them, Katy is back in the Square

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: A loud-mouthed, abrasive American comes to stay at the hotel and soon exposes Basil's ignorance of transatlantic cuisine - particularly Waldorf salad. Classic comedy, starring John Cleese, Prunella Scales and Andrew Sachs

programme.title: The Pact
programme.desc: When a young brewery boss is found dead, a chain of events is triggered that draws four of his employees into a fragile pact of silence that will change their lives for ever. Anna and her friends Nancy, Louie and Cat work at the brewery under young boss Jack Evans, whose bullying, spite and manipulation has taken its toll. After too many drinks at a work function, the women make a snap decision to bundle Jack into a car and dump him in the woods as a prank - but soon after, Jack is found dead. Starring Laura Fraser, Eiry Thomas, Heledd Gwynn and Julie Hesmondhalgh

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: Starstruck
programme.desc: Kate receives an invitation to Tom's premiere and forces a reluctant Jessie to tag along. Tom and Jessie are interrupted by an unexpected visitor

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Morgan is desperate to know if ex Josh wanted an open relationship. With the truth finally out could they have a future together? Tasheeka thought she'd found her perfect match in Dexter until he suddenly ghosted her. He's ready to reveal the truth but will it be too much to swallow? Childhood sweethearts Daniel and Scarlett were plain sailing until he left her to go travelling. Could there be any way back for these lovebirds?

programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple from Scotland look for a property in the Cotswolds on a budget of £600,000, and drops in on show jumping double Olympic gold medallist Nick Skelton

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is at Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey with experts Nell Wragg and Sarah Peterson, who restore an unwanted windsurf sail, a chest of drawers, and a child's cot

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Newbie Irita Marriott brings Latvian flair to the game as she takes on Tim Medhurst in a sporting tour of the antiques emporia of Lincolnshire. One expert has a brush with the law

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Molly is taken aback to learn about Bernie's surrogacy plan and asks her not to go through with it, for the sake of their relationship. Denise bumps into a familiar face down at the allotments, and Sharon tells Zack to stop bringing women back to the flat. Nancy encourages Mick to finish his victim statement and read it in court himself - but unknown to them, Katy is back in the Square

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: A loud-mouthed, abrasive American comes to stay at the hotel and soon exposes Basil's ignorance of transatlantic cuisine - particularly Waldorf salad. Classic comedy, starring John Cleese, Prunella Scales and Andrew Sachs

programme.title: The Pact
programme.desc: When a young brewery boss is found dead, a chain of events is triggered that draws four of his employees into a fragile pact of silence that will change their lives for ever. Anna and her friends Nancy, Louie and Cat work at the brewery under young boss Jack Evans, whose bullying, spite and manipulation has taken its toll. After too many drinks at a work function, the women make a snap decision to bundle Jack into a car and dump him in the woods as a prank - but soon after, Jack is found dead. Starring Laura Fraser, Eiry Thomas, Heledd Gwynn and Julie Hesmondhalgh

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Delving into the world of tanning addicts, who readily turn to illegal melanin injections and nasal sprays - anything to achieve that perfect 'glow'. This films asks what fuels people's fixation with being bronzed and whether it's putting their health at serious risk

programme.title: Starstruck
programme.desc: Kate receives an invitation to Tom's premiere and forces a reluctant Jessie to tag along. Tom and Jessie are interrupted by an unexpected visitor

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race Down Under
programme.desc: The Queens are split in two, as they compete in the ultimate Girl Group sing-off, as the quest to become Down Under's first Drag Race Superstar continues

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Heather Small, as they assist Caitlin and Kristen from Warrington in picking out the best singers from a line-up that includes a singing wrestler, a model and a dentist

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Julia Somerville and Gloria Hunniford take on the fraudsters leaving people thousands of pounds out of pocket, look at the big-name companies who have left some of their customers hanging and present expert advice to help make sure consumers get the best deal available

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin, Martel Maxwell and Lucy Alexander follow a four-year project to develop land in Maidstone, Kent, plus properties in Burnley and Meir, Staffordshire

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: The team investigate a wealthy manager who has been taking advantage of the NHS, and the million-pound tax fraudster who claimed to be a spy

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A teenager fights to save her father's life when he has a heart attack, and a driver loses control of a car, scooping two men up on to the bonnet and crashing through a shop window

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Caroline Hawley guide two teams scouring Spitalfields Market in the heart of London's East End for bargains to take to Catherine Southon's auction house. Plus, presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out how the area around Spitalfields became known as Weaver's Town with TV historian Dan Cruickshank

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid makes preparations for the big apple Sale, while Karen is excited by the prospect of another foster child. Luca gets involved with a patient undergoing superhero therapy

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Contestants Helena, Andy, Carly and Tony vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to win a cash prize. Quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A musical couple from London need Steve Brown's help to unearth a new home in Oxfordshire. On the £950,000 shopping list is a detached property with space for a studio and good links to the capital. Away from the search, Steve checks out a dairy farm where he samples kefir, purportedly an ancient, health-giving drink made from fermented milk

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades saves an old radio, a set of chairs and a side table and chest of drawers from being thrown in the tip, hoping he and two others can make money by restoring the items

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The antiques rivals arrive in Suffolk, where Tim Medhurst likes the look of an art nouveau piece, while Irita Marriott learns why the local cottages are pink

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Frankie is shocked to see Katy at the club and feels conflicted when her mother asks her for a character reference. Martin is annoyed by Zack's constant flirting with Ruby - and even more annoyed when Lily tells him the pair of them spent a lot of time together while he was away. Bernie is under pressure to come up with money for her family and a mystery man arrives in the Square looking for Sonia. Brian Conley joins the cast

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: A major incident strikes Holby and with pupils from Hanssen's old school fighting for life, Josh worries that the CEO is struggling to cope. Will he be brave enough to ask for help before it's too late? Secret addict Kian's focus is frayed when asked to choose between a patient and surrogate son Andrei, and inexperienced doctor Jeong is thrown in at the deep end when Dom asks him to diagnose a mystery condition

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate a scam in which a woman ended up £20,000 out of pocket after beginning an online relationship with a US Navy captain

programme.title: The Pact
programme.desc: As the police begin interviewing the brewery workers about Jack's background and his behaviour at the party, the women go to extraordinary lengths to protect their dark secret. However, someone else knows what happened that night and is now holding them to ransom. Drama, starring Laura Fraser, Julie Hesmondhalgh, Eiry Thomas and Heledd Gwynn, with Eddie Marsan and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: From Bobby Storey's funeral to protests like Black Lives Matter, the response to Covid-19 posed unexpected questions about the Police Service of Northern Ireland. As society emerges from lockdown restrictions, Jennifer O'Leary investigates how the pandemic - along with the Irish Sea border - has exposed tensions over policing

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the opening matches in the penultimate round of Premier League fixtures, including Chelsea v Leicester City at Stamford Bridge. Chelsea and the Foxes were facing each other for the second time in four days, having also met in the FA Cup final, and this contest could have proved to be just as important as the clubs jostled for positions inside the top four. Plus, Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester City, Manchester United v Fulham and Southampton v Leeds United

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Morgan is desperate to know if ex Josh wanted an open relationship. With the truth finally out could they have a future together? Tasheeka thought she'd found her perfect match in Dexter until he suddenly ghosted her. He's ready to reveal the truth but will it be too much to swallow? Childhood sweethearts Daniel and Scarlett were plain sailing until he left her to go travelling. Could there be any way back for these lovebirds?

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norma turns to Romero for guidance when she finds herself in an unexpected position of power. As the pair push the limits with the powerful Bob Paris, Norman and Dylan both come undone at the fallout of a secret, while Caleb considers an offer made to him by a surprising source

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Emma grows closer to Dylan when they both go to Norman's aid. Meanwhile, Norma begins to consider a life away from White Pine Bay

programme.title: Scottish Parliament
programme.desc: All 129 members of the sixth Scottish Parliament taking their oath or affirmation inside the Holyrood chamber, from Thursday 13 May

programme.title: Scottish Parliament

programme.title: Football, Racism and Social Media
programme.desc: For four days, the footballing world, with support from across sport, used its voice - by staying silent on social media. The boycott was the latest attempt at tackling the racist abuse suffered by black and Asian players. Yet even those at the centre of the campaign conceded little would change as a result. Nesta McGregor speaks to victims, social media companies and those charged with cleaning up the sport. Why is the abuse happening, what can be done to stop it and what are the consequences?

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: An undercover investigation inside a lab analysing thousands of Covid-19 tests a day. Secretly filmed footage reveals a failing service with shoddy practices where staff complain they are under pressure to meet targets despite the lab often running well below capacity. The programme discovers there have been three outbreaks of coronavirus among staff and that social distancing is poorly maintained. Test samples sometimes arrive poorly packaged and labelled, with equipment frequently malfunctioning, leading to contamination of results. The programme also discovers that tests, including some intended to find new variants of coronavirus, have been wrongly discarded or lost

programme.desc: Johnny Vegas, Aljaz Skorjanec and Joel Corry join Martin and Roman Kemp for more celebrity chat, music and comedy, as well as games and light-hearted challenges in the which the father-and-son duo go up against their guests

programme.title: Australia with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia continues her 12,000km journey across the continent with a tour of the Northern Territory. She embarks on a crocodile safari in the wetlands of the Northern Territory, before relaxing in Darwin, home of the Ghan train. She then hits the open road with a retiree who now travels the country - or as the Aussies call them 'grey nomads' - to discover life on one of Australia's vast cattle stations

programme.title: Alan Titchmarsh: Spring into Summer
programme.desc: Alan celebrates the great outdoors, offering tips on gardening and farming and getting celebrity guests involved in planting schemes and food tasting. Call the Midwife star Cliff Parisi tells Alan about his role on the show, Camilla Bassett Smith is back in her cottage garden and Jo Behari transforms an old garden bench

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner return with the culinary show, beginning with a host of dishes that can work for breakfast, lunch or even dinner. Lisa prepares bread and butter pudding made with pain au chocolat, while John rustles up a rhubarb cocktail and gives an omelette masterclass

programme.title: Game of Talents
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the game show, with two contestants pairing up with Nick Grimshaw and Clara Amfo to try to win big money by uncovering the secret talents of a range of mystery performers

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Barry in the Vale of Glamorgan for the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: Australian Ninja Warrior
programme.desc: Rebecca Maddern, Ben Fordham and Freddie Flintoff present the Down Under version of the show in which contestants take on a formidable assault course in Melbourne, with obstacles including the Floating Steps, the Swing Surfer, the Tuning Forks, Rolling Steel, Spinball Wizard and the Double Warped Walls

programme.title: Peter Pan (2003)
programme.desc: The adventures of the boy who never grows old, as he whisks Wendy Darling and her brothers away to the magical world of Neverland - where they come face to face with fearsome Captain Hook. Adaptation of JM Barrie's fantasy novel, starring Jeremy Sumpter, Rachel Hurd-Wood, Jason Isaacs and Lynn Redgrave

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Ruth Davidson, Mark King, Ferne McCann and Dan Walker pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for charity. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard presents a celebrity edition of the quiz, with the Rev Richard Coles, Toyah Willcox and Sharron Davies answering questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: Fantastic Beasts and Where to Find Them (2016)
programme.desc: British wizard Newt Scamander arrives in 1920s New York City with a bagful of magical creatures as part of a global study of fantastic beasts, but gets caught up in a conflict when some of the creatures in his care are accidentally released after an inadvertent switching of suitcases with a baker. With a strange, dark force terrorising the Big Apple, Newt needs to find some allies to help him round up the beasts, while saving his own skin in the meantime. Harry Potter prequel written for the screen by JK Rowling, starring Eddie Redmayne, with Katherine Waterston, Colin Farrell, Samantha Morton, Jon Voight, Dan Fogler and Carmen Ejogo

programme.title: The Queen and Her Cousins with Alexander Armstrong
programme.desc: As the Queen turns 95, Alexander Armstrong embarks on a royal road trip of a lifetime. He travels across the country to meet Elizabeth II's cousins, who share what it means to be part of this extraordinary family. Using the monarch's vast family tree as his road map, Alexander meets relatives from many branches of her family - from the ancient lineage of the Mountbatten dynasty, to the Queen Mother's Bowes-Lyon side - and an international princess

programme.title: Trevor McDonald's Indian Train Adventure
programme.desc: Part two of two. The newsreader continues his eight-day journey across India aboard luxury train the Maharajas Express, which recreates the heritage of India's richest kings. On the second leg of the trip, Trevor discovers the reasons why India is rapidly overtaking Britain as an economic superpower. He also visits an unusual temple where people worship rats, finds out how the Taj Mahal is being damaged by the effects of pollution, and concludes his journey at the site of Mahatma Gandhi's assassination

programme.title: Trevor McDonald & Charlene White: Has George Floyd Changed Britain?
programme.desc: Approaching the one-year anniversary of the death of George Floyd, Trevor McDonald and Charlene White explore the impact for people living in the UK. This documentary investigates the consequences of the tragedy, and the conversations that have followed in Britain

programme.desc: Julia continues her 12,000km journey across the continent with a tour of the Northern Territory. She embarks on a crocodile safari in the wetlands of the Northern Territory, before relaxing in Darwin, home of the Ghan train. She then hits the open road with a retiree who now travels the country - or as the Aussies call them 'grey nomads' - to discover life on one of Australia's vast cattle stations

programme.title: Alan Titchmarsh: Spring into Summer
programme.desc: Alan celebrates the great outdoors, offering tips on gardening and farming and getting celebrity guests involved in planting schemes and food tasting. Call the Midwife star Cliff Parisi tells Alan about his role on the show, Camilla Bassett Smith is back in her cottage garden and Jo Behari transforms an old garden bench

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner return with the culinary show, beginning with a host of dishes that can work for breakfast, lunch or even dinner. Lisa prepares bread and butter pudding made with pain au chocolat, while John rustles up a rhubarb cocktail and gives an omelette masterclass

programme.title: Game of Talents
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the game show, with two contestants pairing up with Nick Grimshaw and Clara Amfo to try to win big money by uncovering the secret talents of a range of mystery performers

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Barry in the Vale of Glamorgan for the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: Australian Ninja Warrior
programme.desc: Rebecca Maddern, Ben Fordham and Freddie Flintoff present the Down Under version of the show in which contestants take on a formidable assault course in Melbourne, with obstacles including the Floating Steps, the Swing Surfer, the Tuning Forks, Rolling Steel, Spinball Wizard and the Double Warped Walls

programme.title: Peter Pan (2003)
programme.desc: The adventures of the boy who never grows old, as he whisks Wendy Darling and her brothers away to the magical world of Neverland - where they come face to face with fearsome Captain Hook. Adaptation of JM Barrie's fantasy novel, starring Jeremy Sumpter, Rachel Hurd-Wood, Jason Isaacs and Lynn Redgrave

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Ruth Davidson, Mark King, Ferne McCann and Dan Walker pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for charity. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard presents a celebrity edition of the quiz, with the Rev Richard Coles, Toyah Willcox and Sharron Davies answering questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: Fantastic Beasts and Where to Find Them (2016)
programme.desc: British wizard Newt Scamander arrives in 1920s New York City with a bagful of magical creatures as part of a global study of fantastic beasts, but gets caught up in a conflict when some of the creatures in his care are accidentally released after an inadvertent switching of suitcases with a baker. With a strange, dark force terrorising the Big Apple, Newt needs to find some allies to help him round up the beasts, while saving his own skin in the meantime. Harry Potter prequel written for the screen by JK Rowling, starring Eddie Redmayne, with Katherine Waterston, Colin Farrell, Samantha Morton, Jon Voight, Dan Fogler and Carmen Ejogo

programme.title: The Queen and Her Cousins with Alexander Armstrong
programme.desc: As the Queen turns 95, Alexander Armstrong embarks on a royal road trip of a lifetime. He travels across the country to meet Elizabeth II's cousins, who share what it means to be part of this extraordinary family. Using the monarch's vast family tree as his road map, Alexander meets relatives from many branches of her family - from the ancient lineage of the Mountbatten dynasty, to the Queen Mother's Bowes-Lyon side - and an international princess

programme.title: Trevor McDonald's Indian Train Adventure
programme.desc: Part two of two. The newsreader continues his eight-day journey across India aboard luxury train the Maharajas Express, which recreates the heritage of India's richest kings. On the second leg of the trip, Trevor discovers the reasons why India is rapidly overtaking Britain as an economic superpower. He also visits an unusual temple where people worship rats, finds out how the Taj Mahal is being damaged by the effects of pollution, and concludes his journey at the site of Mahatma Gandhi's assassination

programme.title: Trevor McDonald & Charlene White: Has George Floyd Changed Britain?
programme.desc: Approaching the one-year anniversary of the death of George Floyd, Trevor McDonald and Charlene White explore the impact for people living in the UK. This documentary investigates the consequences of the tragedy, and the conversations that have followed in Britain

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The collapse of a seemingly airtight prosecution case reflects badly on the detective, and it is not long before the media are baying for his blood. To make matters worse, a previous investigation comes back to haunt him, and Lane gets caught in the crossfire. Drama, starring Jack Shepherd, Jimmy Yuill and Helen Masters

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A prostitute's client confesses to the murder of another working girl before vanishing into the night, leaving Wycliffe with the tricky task of tracking him down before he kills again. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The discovery of a woman's body in a car park presents Jack Frost with another challenge to his deductive powers. Delving into the victim's electronic organiser, he learns she was connected with a local religious community - and realises someone in the congregation is more interested in the pleasures of this world than the rewards of the next. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander and John Lyons

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Millionaire Gordon Cloade's wife is widowed for the second time at the tender age of 23, arousing the suspicions of his impoverished relatives. Fanning the flames of envy and hatred, psychic aunt Kathy Cloade reveals the grieving widow's first husband is in fact still alive, and a second death prompts Poirot to delve deeper into the family's dark secrets. David Suchet stars, with Jenny Agutter, Tim Piggot-Smith and Celia Imrie

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A cleaner is found dead in her home and suspicion immediately falls upon her lodger, who is arrested and convicted of her murder. It appears to be an open-and-shut case, but Superintendent Spence begins to question the man's guilt and invites Poirot to help uncover the identity of the real killer. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips and Raquel Cassidy

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: When dock worker Owen Thorne plunges from the roof of a multi-storey car park while picking up his daughter from her school prom, Vera immediately detects foul play. The team soon discovers a work dispute over the death of a young man, with everyone believing Owen was to blame, and the victim's father admits he wanted revenge - but his alibi holds. The mystery then continues when Vera spots an inconsistency in the statement Owen's wife gave to the police, suggesting she's not certain of her husband's fidelity

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the murder of a trainee forensic psychologist, whose body has been dumped in a Northumberland landfill site, surroundings that make evidence of how she died impossible to verify. Vera discovers that the dead woman had been working for the prison service re-investigating a crime that a former prisoner had been convicted of, and realises she must finish what the victim started in order to discover who may have wanted her dead. Paul Kaye plays a new pathologist alongside fellow guest star Peter Davison

programme.desc: Zoe and Frankie get ready for their trip. Zak and Lisa return to the village, and he has it out with Angie. Kathy tells Laura that she feels more than she should for Graham

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick tries to keep the peace during a feud between neighbours, and Greengrass thinks his luck has changed for the better when he wins first prize in a raffle

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: The doctor and his wife hire a servant, unaware that the lady in question is his long-lost mother

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George seeks solace when his budgie dies, but Mildred is too preoccupied with finding something good enough to donate to the jumble sale. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is invited to attend a masquerade ball dressed as someone famous and modestly goes as himself - and finds time amid the revelry to solve the murder of aristocratic fellow guest Lord Cronshaw. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A climber plunges to his death from a cliff face and a police informant claims it was no accident. A re-enactment of the incident adds fuel to the fire, and Wycliffe braces himself to interview the dead man's best friend. Drama, starring Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters, Lynn Farleigh, Christian Solimeno and Paul Venables

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The town of Aidensfield falls victim to a spate of burglaries - which all seem to be aimed at Maggie

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak urges Butch to show Emily how he feels. Eric pesters Stella about the health farm. Biff tells Graham to take the ring back. Frankie thinks she's offended Zoe

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick questions Alison about Brian's death. Leanne tries to keep Darren occupied and stalls when he tries to get her upstairs. Jim is put on a drip to stop his blood clotting

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim admits to Liz that he is frightened he will never walk again. Greg talks Mike into employing him as a sales consultant. Les appears in court, and Sally receives tragic news

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Schoolgirl Jennifer Sutcliffe becomes embroiled in a mystery when priceless jewels are hidden among her belongings. The precious stones once belonged to a prince who fell foul of a revolution, and now his cousin is a pupil at the same school as Jennifer, where Poirot finds himself drawn into the unlikely situation and realises nothing is quite what it seems. David Suchet stars as the Belgian sleuth, with Harriet Walter and Claire Skinner

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A policeman's murder gives Wycliffe plenty to think about - especially as the chief suspect is a fellow officer who has been having an affair with the victim's wife. Adding to the pressure, senior figures within the force want the matter cleared up as quickly as possible to avoid a scandal. Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: The team assists 39-year-old mother of three Miriam Aragon in her search for her Salvadoran father. After growing up in Wales with her single mother and only finding out about her father's identity at the age of 19, Miriam is desperate to find him. Meanwhile, 48-year-old Annie Sims is determined to find her birth mother, who gave her up for adoption as a baby, but kept her older sister. Following the birth of her own daughters, Annie's compassion for her biological mother has grown, and she hopes to find her and hear her side of the story. Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A blood-spattered hotel room and a stolen credit card lead Ronnie and Joe to Charles Hutton, a member of the wealthy upper classes who dotes on his wife Camille and daughter Georgia. The detectives find clues that suggest Georgia gave birth at the scene, but where is the baby now? The young woman and her boyfriend Rufus prove difficult for Jake and Kate to prosecute, and to make matters worse, the defence counsel turns out to be Jake's old nemesis. As the evidence stacks up, how far will Charles go to protect his privileged family? Guest starring James Wilby and Joseph Millson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Beautiful Russian aristocrat Countess Vera Rossakoff, the chief suspect in a series of jewel thefts, seemingly captivates Poirot with her charm and intellect, meaning Hastings and Miss Lemon have to do most of the actual detective work and ensure she is brought to justice. Drama, guest starring Kika Markham

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A woman's body is pulled from the river, reminding Frost that he investigated the death of her husband's first wife years ago. Unfortunately, it turns out that a key witness's statement went astray during the earlier case - which leads to Frost being suspended from duty. Discreetly continuing his enquiries, he is intrigued to learn the local priest recently heard a very disturbing confession. Starring David Jason, Eoin McCarthy, Anthony Calf, Kenneth Cope and Isla Blair

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A policeman's murder gives Wycliffe plenty to think about - especially as the chief suspect is a fellow officer who has been having an affair with the victim's wife. Adding to the pressure, senior figures within the force want the matter cleared up as quickly as possible to avoid a scandal. Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick has to go undercover following the unlikely event of a gangster setting up shop in rural Aidensfield - and luckily his former girlfriend Jill is able to help out

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Graham fears for Kathy's safety after Biff's display of violence. Emily is excited about her date with Butch. Biff and Kathy have a heart-to-heart about their relationship

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Gavin makes an amorous advance towards Stella. Butch takes Emily to see her dad in hospital. Kathy tells Graham she is determined to marry Biff

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Janice urges Leanne to fight for her marriage. Les accuses Nick of being unfaithful to Leanne. Des wants Samantha to have his baby

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick tells Gail that Leanne wants a divorce. Sally is devastated by her mother's death, but Kevin is more interested in selling off her house. Jim returns home

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective's friend Sir Charles Cartwright (guest star Martin Shaw) hosts a cocktail party at his home in Cornwall, where an amiable local priest chokes to death on his drink. There seems to be no motive for murder, and no trace of poison found in the victim's glass, but weeks later, when Sir Charles's doctor friend Sir Bartholomew Strange dies in similar circumstances, he and Poirot team up to investigate. Drama, with Art Malik, Jane Asher and Anna Carteret, and David Suchet in the title role

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An archaeologist dies of a heart attack shortly after smashing the seal of an ancient Egyptian tomb, raising the spectre of the mummy's curse. The grieving widow summons the Belgian sleuth to unravel the mystery, but he can only smoke out the real villain by risking his own life. David Suchet stars, with Anna Cropper and Bill Bailey

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: An anonymous enemy goes all out to ruin Wycliffe by sending the police a batch of photos apparently showing him taking bribes. As soon as she hears the news, Lane puts an investigation into the deaths of two drug-dealers on hold and leaps to her boss's defence - risking her career in the process. Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When director Nick Cheyney is found beheaded by a guillotine on the set of his latest film, Barnaby and Jones are left with no shortage of suspects. The victim, it transpires, was on poor terms with several people in some way connected to the project, including the mother of his child, and an actor who blamed him for ruining his career. Guest starring Nicky Henson

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a series of shootings claims one of their own, Ronnie and Joe have little time to grieve - they must solve the case before the killer reaches their next target on a hit list that includes CPS director Henry Sharpe. It gradually becomes apparent that the common thread connecting all the names is the case of Mark Glendon, convicted of the murder of his wife four years earlier. Although he's still in custody, a retrial is imminent, putting pressure on the team to catch the murderer before the jury delivers its verdict

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A lavish dinner party proves to be a prelude to murder and intrigue - but fortunately Poirot is among the guests, and soon realises the key to the mystery lies in a duel fought 10 years previously. Drama, starring David Suchet, Caroline Langrishe and John McEnery

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Pawnbroker Peter Lawson is shot dead in an armed robbery at his premises and Frost's investigations unearth a tragic family secret. He is convinced Lawson was killed by one of his children in revenge for years of systematic sexual abuse - but the truth turns out to be even more chilling. David Jason stars, with Diane Keen, Isla Blair, Matt Bardock, Anthony Bate, Mark Lambert and Edward Purver

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: The team turns out for another Saturday game. Comedy, starring Josie Lawrence, Timothy Spall, Robert Daws and Brenda Blethyn

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Jimmy makes a call, Aaron takes matters into his own hands, and Pollard is in trouble

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The collapse of a seemingly airtight prosecution case reflects badly on the detective, and it is not long before the media are baying for his blood. To make matters worse, a previous investigation comes back to haunt him, and Lane gets caught in the crossfire. Drama, starring Jack Shepherd, Jimmy Yuill and Helen Masters

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A prostitute's client confesses to the murder of another working girl before vanishing into the night, leaving Wycliffe with the tricky task of tracking him down before he kills again. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The discovery of a woman's body in a car park presents Jack Frost with another challenge to his deductive powers. Delving into the victim's electronic organiser, he learns she was connected with a local religious community - and realises someone in the congregation is more interested in the pleasures of this world than the rewards of the next. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander and John Lyons

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Millionaire Gordon Cloade's wife is widowed for the second time at the tender age of 23, arousing the suspicions of his impoverished relatives. Fanning the flames of envy and hatred, psychic aunt Kathy Cloade reveals the grieving widow's first husband is in fact still alive, and a second death prompts Poirot to delve deeper into the family's dark secrets. David Suchet stars, with Jenny Agutter, Tim Piggot-Smith and Celia Imrie

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A cleaner is found dead in her home and suspicion immediately falls upon her lodger, who is arrested and convicted of her murder. It appears to be an open-and-shut case, but Superintendent Spence begins to question the man's guilt and invites Poirot to help uncover the identity of the real killer. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips and Raquel Cassidy

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: When dock worker Owen Thorne plunges from the roof of a multi-storey car park while picking up his daughter from her school prom, Vera immediately detects foul play. The team soon discovers a work dispute over the death of a young man, with everyone believing Owen was to blame, and the victim's father admits he wanted revenge - but his alibi holds. The mystery then continues when Vera spots an inconsistency in the statement Owen's wife gave to the police, suggesting she's not certain of her husband's fidelity

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the murder of a trainee forensic psychologist, whose body has been dumped in a Northumberland landfill site, surroundings that make evidence of how she died impossible to verify. Vera discovers that the dead woman had been working for the prison service re-investigating a crime that a former prisoner had been convicted of, and realises she must finish what the victim started in order to discover who may have wanted her dead. Paul Kaye plays a new pathologist alongside fellow guest star Peter Davison

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Amy advises Asha against giving Corey an alibi. Abi quizzes Nina about the attack. Kelly panics as the net closes in on her

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: The duo try to track down an American colonel tormented by memories his harrowing experiences while in command of an air base in Vietnam. Robert Vaughn and Nyree Dawn Porter star

programme.title: Auto Mundial
programme.desc: A look at a hybrid SUV from Honda, and the all-new Volkswagen Golf R

programme.title: British Touring Car Championship Highlights
programme.desc: Steve Rider presents action from the first three races of the campaign from Thruxton. Commentary from David Addison and Tim Harvey, with reprots by Louise Goodman

programme.title: World Superbike Highlights
programme.desc: A look ahead to the 2021 World Superbike season, as the riders go for glory over 13 rounds, beginning in Aragon, Spain. Kawasaki's Jonathan Rea won last year's title.

programme.title: MotoGP Live
programme.desc: The Grand Prix de France. All the action from the Moto3, Moto2 and MotoGP races in the fifth round of the season at Le Mans. Jack Miller won the MotoGP race last time out at the Spanish Grand Prix in Jerez after Yamaha's Fabio Quartararo suffered an arm-pump issue while leading the race and went on to finish in 13th position. Miller's Ducati team mate, Francecso Bagnaia, claimed second place and that was enough for him to overtake Quartararo at the top of the championship standings. With commentary from Steve Day and Matt Birt and pit-lane reports by Simon Crafar

programme.title: Isle of Man TT
programme.desc: A look back at the best stagings over the last 10 years of the meeting's blue riband event, the Senior TT

programme.title: The Unrideables
programme.desc: A look back at motorcycling in the late 1980s and early 1990s, which is widely regarded as one of the most exciting eras in the history of two-wheel racing and featured the likes of Wayne Rainey, Eddie Lawson, Freddie Spencer, Wayne Gardner, Mick Doohan and Ron Haslam. Including action from memorable races that involved these riders competing against each other

programme.title: Rambo: First Blood Part II (1985)
programme.desc: Incarcerated Special Forces veteran John Rambo is offered the chance for freedom by former commander Colonel Trautman if he takes up the offer to return to the jungles of Vietnam on a life-or-death mission to rescue American PoWs. During the course of the operation, he develops strong feelings for his guide, a female freedom fighter - but tragedy strikes, leaving him more determined than ever to exact a bloody and explosive revenge. Action adventure sequel, starring Sylvester Stallone, Richard Crenna, Julia Nickson-Soul, Steven Berkoff, George Kee Cheung, Andy Wood and Martin Kove

programme.title: Rambo: First Blood Part II (1985)
programme.desc: Incarcerated Special Forces veteran John Rambo is offered the chance for freedom by former commander Colonel Trautman if he takes up the offer to return to the jungles of Vietnam on a life-or-death mission to rescue American PoWs. programme.title: Jeremy Wade's Mighty Rivers
programme.desc: Biologist, extreme angler and naturalist Jeremy Wade heads to the Amazon. Together with its tributaries, it contains an incredible 20 per cent of Earth's fresh water, and the immense forests it sustains generate a quarter of the oxygen needed for life on the planet. Disgorging more water than the next nine biggest rivers combined, this waterway is indeed a mighty affair. By tracing the epic migration of the dorado catfish, Jeremy discovers the issues putting this great river at risk - from leaking pipelines to giant dams. However, he also discovers tales of surprising hope

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The guys invite the girls to a Dungeons and Dragons night when their trip to Vegas falls through after an incident with airport security. However, following a few drinks, Sheldon and Amy's relationship takes an unexpected turn. Meanwhile, Raj goes on a date with Lucy, who tries to sneak out of the restaurant's bathroom window - but ends up getting stuck and calling for his help

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer is persuaded to go undercover to win the trust of Fat Tony as part of an FBI operation to bring the mobster down after an incident involving recycling responsibilities and bribery. With the voice of Jon Hamm (Mad Men)

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Tension between Springfield Elementary's fourth and fifth graders spills over during a dodgeball game, leading Bart to arrange a fight with a fellow pupil. Marge gets back in touch with three friends she fell out with years ago, but she soon realises why they went their separate ways

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer are joined by Clare Balding, Jack Whitehall, Suggs, James Newman and Laura Donnelly. Plus, singer Griff performs in the studio

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Moe achieves massive success when he turns the tavern into a gay bar, but keeps his hetrosexuality a secret to remain in his new clientele's favour. However, the decision backfires when the pub's new regulars encourage him to run for office and become Springfield's first openly gay councilman. Meanwhile, Principal Skinner falls for a new music teacher. Guest starring Kristen Wiig (Bridesmaids) and Alyson Hannigan (How I Met Your Mother)

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer becomes obsessed with a father-son relationship in a 1980s sitcom, using it as a source of inspiration to deal with Bart's hankering for a must-have mini-bike. However, when the youngster starts trading nuclear secrets with the Chinese government, Homer must find a new way to bring his son into line

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge notices grey hairs creeping through her blue dye and decides to embrace the shade like other middle-aged women. Homer becomes Moe's official 'wing man' in a bid to boost his friend's ailing love life

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Ned Flanders takes in a pair of female lodgers - who start broadcasting raunchy videos over the internet. Ashamed to show his face around Springfield, he leaves town - but the Simpsons' new neighbour is not quite what they were hoping for. Jason Bateman guest stars

programme.title: The Adventures of Tintin (2011)
programme.desc: A reporter's purchase of a model ship brings him under threat from a ruthless criminal, as it is one of a series of clues to the location of the real vessel, which holds a pirate's lost treasure. He sets off on a globetrotting quest to find it, assisted by a drunken sea captain, two bumbling detectives and his faithful dog. Steven Spielberg's animated adventure based on Herge's comic-strip series, with the voices of Jamie Bell, Andy Serkis and Daniel Craig

programme.title: Wonder Park (2019)
programme.desc: June, a science-loving little girl, and her mother spend hours playing with stuffed animals and other toys, fashioning a world of imagination connected to a fabulous theme park. When June's mom is diagnosed with a life-threatening illness and has to go away for treatment, June finds a real version of her theme park. Animated family adventure, with the voices of Mila Kunis and Jennifer Garner

programme.title: 50 Years of Mr Men with Matt Lucas
programme.desc: Actor, comedian and superfan Matt Lucas charts the history of the colourful little characters who found their way into the hearts and bedtime stories of generations of children around the world. It's a story that began with a 'tickle' in 1971 and became a family dynasty - and one of Britain's most pioneering and enduring global brands

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Titanic: Into the Heart of the Wreck
programme.desc: Capturing the underwater odyssey of the greatest Titanic expeditions and the evolution of the most scrutinised shipwreck in the world. From the epic discovery of the wreck by a French-American team in 1985 to the exploratory missions led by James Cameron, who managed to light the wreck scene like a Hollywood film set and venture inside the ship thanks to remote-controlled robots, this documentary captures the unequalled thrill of those extreme dives

programme.title: SAS: Who Dares Wins
programme.desc: After a physically and psychologically intense introduction to their SAS selection, the obstacle for the 17 remaining recruits to overcome over the next 48 hours is fear, as they are sent deeper into the wild and unforgiving terrain of the Isle of Raasay in Scotland. They are put through their paces in a forward abseil, exposed to extreme cold in a water immersion exercise and required to operate as a team in a pitch-black disused tunnel

programme.title: Fargo
programme.desc: The Smutnys receive unexpected guests, Gaetano arrives in Kansas City from the old country, Josto adjusts to his new role, and Oraetta is caught red-handed during her routine ministrations. Meanwhile, Detective Odis Weff investigates a hospital shooting, and Loy conducts a test that challenges the status quo. Chris Rock stars in this black comedy drama, inspired by the Coen brothers' Oscar-winning film

programme.title: Time Is a Killer
programme.desc: French psychological thriller series, interweaving the summers of 1994 and 2019 to piece together the events leading up to a tragic car accident on Corsica, that left Clotilde Baron as the sole survivor in her family at the age of 16. Starring Mathilde Seigner, Caterina Murino, Jenifer Bartoli, Gregory Fitoussi, Thierry Godard, Stanley Weber, Valeria Cavalli, Serge Riaboukine and Fred Testot

programme.title: The Counsellor (2013)
programme.desc: A lawyer who earns a living representing drug dealers makes a fateful decision to take an active role in their criminal activities. He quickly finds himself out of his depth in a criminal underworld he barely understands, where his actions bring deadly consequences. Ridley Scott's crime thriller, starring Michael Fassbender, Penelope Cruz, Cameron Diaz, Javier Bardem and Brad Pitt

programme.title: The Handmaid's Tale
programme.desc: Confined in a hospital, June's sanity begins to fray, but an encounter with Serena Joy forces June to reassess her recent actions. Drama, starring Elisabeth Moss and Yvonne Strahovski

In New Zealand, George visits a hilltop beach house overlooking the Pacific, and as ever he and old friend Will Hardie embark on another series-long project - this time to build a gravity-defying, rotating home inspired by vintage sci-fi comics

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me

programme.desc: Ray and Frank are pressured by their wives to join Robert in one of his therapy sessions - but the three of them decide to go to the racetrack instead
