programme.desc: The shrink bumps into a former friend of Martin's while visiting Maris in hospital. The pair parted on bad terms, so Frasier tries to effect a reconciliation. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and John Mahoney

They spend money faster than it comes in and mum Anna, who can't resist a bargain has a cosmetics drawer that rivals any professional make-up artist's. Dad Mark has fallen out of love with DIY and their youngest son refuses to eat the same evening family meal, which means hundreds of pounds is wasted on unnecessary cooking

programme.title: Catching Paedophiles: Crime and Punishment
programme.desc: Film exploring the challenges of investigating and prosecuting child sexual abuse, and how to manage predators when they've been caught. Detective Claire Lyons, who works for Hampshire Constabulary's Internet Child Abuse Team, has to prove that her suspicions are right about a man who has been uploading indecent images of children - and not risk any mistakes that would lead to the suspect walking free and being able to offend again

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: With two thirds of all British Covid deaths coming after September 2020, Dispatches investigates why, and examines what role the Prime Minister's decisions of last Autumn played in there being a massive second surge in infections that saw tens of thousands of people die. As Boris Johnson's former chief advisor Dominic Cummings threatens to reveal all, reporter Antony Barnett hears from some at the heart of Downing Street discussions, and learns why the Prime Minister ignored government scientific advice and continued to battle against another lockdown. Dispatches also probes the Prime Minister's border plans to stop virus variants coming into the UK and hears from a whistleblower who fears they've failed to do the job

programme.title: Dan Snow: The Tutankhamun Mystery
programme.desc: The historian explores the story of the Egyptian pharaoh's life and death. He encounters the mummies of the boy king's parents in Cairo, investigates an injury the pharaoh sustained towards the end of his life - an injury that would eventually result in his death - and explores why images of the king look more like a woman

programme.title: Cher and the Loneliest Elephant
programme.desc: Documentary following the singer and her charity Free the Wild as they help to rescue an Asian elephant from the Islamabad Zoo and relocate it to an animal reserve in Cambodia

programme.title: Bargain Loving Brits by the Sea
programme.desc: Daisy and Matt from Skegness seal sanctuary Natureland try to boost visitor numbers to help fund their work rescuing seal pups in distress along the Lincolnshire coast. At the Harbourside Gift Shop in Scarborough, owner Dawn Jackson's son Miles is convinced visitors will flock to try out his seagull-flavoured rock, but his mum is sceptical

programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: The singer concludes her journey around the Caribbean, visiting Grand Cayman for a spot of horse-riding along the beach. She then heads to Roatan off the coast of Honduras, discovering the wonders of the deep aboard a submarine, before finishing her trip on the Mexican island of Cozumel, where she parties the night away at the colourful local carnival

programme.title: Gibraltar: Britain in the Sun
programme.desc: Head of ape management Eric Shaw has trouble capturing a macaque, a group of visiting university students descends 200 metres into the depths of St Michael's Cave and bad weather threatens the opening of Gibraltar's new £65million airport terminal

programme.title: Britain's Favourite 90s Songs
programme.desc: Revisiting the musical highs of the decade, counting down the official top 30 biggest-selling singles in each year and looking at songs that didn't make the chart but left a lasting impression. The first edition examines 1990, with Chesney Hawkes, Shaun Ryder, Jayne Middlemiss, Paul Gambaccini, Anthea Turner, Bez and other famous faces guiding viewers through the biggest hits of the year, including Nothing Compares 2 U, Killer, The Power and U Can't Touch This

programme.title: Wonderful World of Chocolate
programme.desc: Documentary offering a glimpse inside the confectionery world, visiting factories, chocolatiers and shops, and even revealing the Queen's favourite chocolates

programme.title: Eamonn & Ruth's 7 Year Itch
programme.desc: The second of two programmes in which Ruth Langsford and Eamonn Holmes, having celebrated their seventh wedding anniversary, meet various other couples to find out how to keep their marriage alive. They include a family where the husband waited seven years to announce out of the blue he wanted to be a woman, and a couple who use fantasy role play to escape the pressure of raising two autistic children. They also explore meet a pair of swingers and two Indian childhood sweethearts who defied the custom of arranged marriage

programme.title: Now That's Funny!

programme.title: Divine Designs

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Architect Dermot Bannon helps Avril and Andrew Wilson-Rankin, who have dreamt of owning their own home since they first met 10 years previously. The couple have a budget of 260,000 euros to transform a two-storey disused commercial lock-up on a side street in Dun Laoghaire, Dublin, into an urban dwelling complete with exposed brickwork, and internal bridge and a mezzanine level. However, the logistical challenge of working on a restricted site quickly puts the budget and tight schedule under pressure

The pair plan to open a chilli farm, shop and restaurant on the site, but things take a drastic turn for the worse just one month into the project

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: West Midlander Paul has high hopes for his recently opened cafe bar offering fry-ups and Sunday lunch among the tapas and tavernas of the city's historic old town. Scottish couple Dawn-Cherie and Tom bought a caravan online four weeks ago, only for the campsite to have no record of the sale, 51-year-old Karen sets up a class for British people to learn Spanish, and Sue and Rod have opened a horse rescue centre

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Bill Nighy narrates a trip through Wales' highest peaks and deepest valleys. Starting in Pwllheli in the north west, the route heads along the lines that were built during the slate industry's heyday. At Porthmadog, the journey briefly switches to the reconstructed steam trains of the Welsh Highland National Heritage Railway to Minffordd station. Nearby is the village of Portmeirion, famous for its role in the classic TV series The Prisoner. Rejoining the Cambrian Line, local farmers reveal what the railway means to them, before the route takes in the seaside resort of Aberdovey

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: PC Andy Swift is patrolling on the M1 when a car he has flagged down takes off, prompting a chase at speeds up to 120mph, while PC Jason Potts is called to a pub where a distressed woman is convinced her boyfriend has fallen into the freezing waters of a canal

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: A patient ignores hospital policy and refuses to leave A&E after his treatment and staff and security end up in the firing line when he lashes out aggressively, while doctor Sean assesses 27-year-old Chris, who lost control of his motorbike and crushed his leg

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: One of the department's new junior doctors treats a man with a suspected case of sepsis, that has been triggered by the tracheostomy tube he relies on to breathe. An 84-year-old woman is brought in by ambulance after falling down a flight of stairs and undergoes multiple x-rays to check for broken bones, while a 12-month-old baby needs treatment after having a fit

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red
programme.desc: A West Midlands critical care team is called to a road traffic accident in which a 13-year-old boy has been knocked over on the way to school, and now has blood coming out of one ear

programme.title: Extraordinary People
programme.desc: The story of a two-year-old Vietnamese boy born with one of the world's rarest facial deformities. In addition to a cleft palate, Viet has a two-inch gap in the bone on each side of his face that will increase as he grows, and his condition does not just affect his appearance - he is also blind in both eyes. This documentary follows a British medical team led by surgeon Niall Kirkpatrick, who try to rebuild Viet's face in a series of extremely challenging operations

programme.title: How to Get a Good Night's Sleep
programme.desc: First of a two-part documentary in which Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford set out to cure their respective sleeping disorders - lack of sleep and snoring. They start out by road-testing a few expensive beds to find out if paying more is worth it, before Ruth joins forces with a woman whose snoring sends her husband to the spare room every night. Together they try out a few aids designed to prevent their night-time noise. Meanwhile, Eamonn and fellow insomniac Sam work out a sleep programme that focuses on establishing a set routine

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Jean Robinson and Daithi Elliffe seek the help of architect Dermot Bannon to turn a listed 19th-century former schoolhouse at Kilternan, which has scenic views of the Dublin mountains, into their ideal family home. The property has been virtually disused for the past 30 years and is dank and dilapidated, with rotting floors, and the new homeowners are seeking a comfortable and bright dwelling with a modern layout. However, despite a generous budget of 360,000 euros, the constraints of a protected structure, a tight schedule and hidden costs begin to take their toll on the project

programme.title: Killer Sentence: Hailey Dean Mysteries (2019)
programme.desc: Therapist and sleuth Hailey Dean knows District Attorney Paulina D'Orazio is innocent of the murder of a man she once put behind bars and must do all she can to prove it in the face of mounting evidence. Mystery drama, starring Kellie Martin, Viv Leacock, and Lauren Holly

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Bar manager Chris and hairdresser Gary have only been in Benidorm a week, but they have already found the perfect apartment and are now looking for work. But Chris has something else on his mind, which involves asking his boyfriend a special question. The documentary also catches up with Linda, who runs a dog rescue charity, and introduces Bradford-born Chris, who has turned his love of buying-and-selling into opening an antique and second-hand furniture shop

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Christian finds himself drugged, strapped to a bed, and faced with a murderous Lewis. Bella tries to find a quiet space to finish her assignment

programme.title: 5 News Tonight
programme.desc: A round-up of the evening's headlines, including coverage of the latest national and international stories

programme.title: Dream Kitchens and Bathrooms with Mark Millar
programme.desc: The DIY expert comes to the aid of homeowners who are transforming the most important rooms in the house. In this episode, Mark meets Adam in Yorkshire, who wants to transform the bathroom in his rundown country cottage, featuring a vaulted ceiling, wood panelling, stone flooring and a vintage bath. Elsewhere in Walthamstow, Emma and Jono hope to create a Japanese-inspired designer kitchen, and turn to Mark for advice

programme.title: The Yorkshire Vet
programme.desc: A ewe in labour is rushed into the animal hospital from Cannon Hall Farm, and vet Matt prepares to deal with this urgent case in the car park. Meanwhile, Peter has a reunion with a feisty feline, and Morris the beagle is rushed into the practice by his worried owners after suffering a sudden illness

programme.title: Our Yorkshire Farm
programme.desc: The Owen family try their hand at self-sufficiency with their very first dairy cow, and Reuben accompanies his mum and younger sisters to pick up Buttercup from a neighbouring farm. Back at the farm, the family take on the toughest job ahead of the imminent breeding season, rounding up the free-roaming ewes who have spread themselves across hundreds of acres of wild moorland

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: Ben travels to the coastal forests of southern Sweden to meet 24-year-old single mother Annalisa, a native Italian who left everything in her home country behind when she was a teenager, and is now developing a forest hideaway on the fringes of society for her and her toddler. Ben helps her chop wood and make her home baby-proof, while Annalisa goes busking to make a small living. They also go dumpster-diving in search of rubbish that can be put to good use to counter the amount of waste thrown away

programme.title: Pompeii: The Bodies in the Basement
programme.desc: A new investigation into one of the most harrowing gravesites of the 79AD eruption, where more than 55 bodies were found cowering in a warehouse at a site that has long baffled archaeologists. While the skeletons at the back of the room have no possessions, those crowded at the front are laden with jewellery and gold - but why, in the chaos of their dying moments, were the bodies so starkly divided by wealth?

programme.title: Rogue Earth: When Mother Nature Takes Revenge
programme.desc: Using video captured on smartphones and other devices, this programme recounts the Super Outbreak of 2011, when 362 tornadoes ripped across 21 American states. The story is told by heroes, survivors and victims in Alabama, where 252 fatalities and millions of dollars in property damage occurred

programme.title: Entertainment News on 5
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: How to Get a Good Night's Sleep
programme.desc: Part two of two. Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford continue their mission to cure their sleeping disorders. Eamonn is given a new sleep programme by top sports scientist Nick Littlehales, which requires that he sleeps in short bursts over 24 hours. Next, the couple investigate two luxury hotel 'sleep packages', before testing out a sleep pod designed to allow workers 20 minute naps

programme.title: The Funny Thing About
programme.desc: Johnny Vegas teams up with a variety of well-known British stars to take an irreverent look back at the process of growing into adulthood. Suzanne Shaw and Megan McKenna offer up tales of embarrassing accidents up against a tree and behind a sofa respectively, while Ian 'H' Watkins explains how he made Gary Barlow suffer a similar misfortune. Plus, an account from a celebrity whose mother used their head to crack eggs, another whose treasure hunt unearthed a secret stash of condoms, and one who went to school with a boy nicknamed 'Hard-on Harvey'

programme.title: Wildlife SOS
programme.desc: The work of volunteers treating sick, injured and orphaned animals at a wildlife sanctuary

programme.title: Divine Designs
programme.desc: Paul Binski, Professor of the History of Medieval Art at Cambridge University, visits Chelsea's Gothic-revival Holy Trinity Church designed by John Dando Sedding, and William Lethaby's thatched All Saints' Church in Brockhampton, Herefordshire, which boasts fittings by William Morris and Edward Burne-Jones of the Arts and Crafts movement

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Ann Maurice helps a chauffeur and his girlfriend spruce up their neglected seaside bungalow in Clacton-on-Sea, Essex, to make it more attractive to potential buyers, transforming the interior and landscaping the overgrown gardens. Presented by Alistair Appleton

programme.title: Rango (2011)
programme.desc: A pet chameleon with an active imagination ends up lost in the desert. He makes his way to a lawless town of animals, where he is mistaken for a deadly gunslinger and made sheriff. He organises the townsfolk into a posse when bandits led by an evil rattlesnake steal their supply of water. Oscar-winning animated comedy, with the voices of Johnny Depp, Isla Fisher and Bill Nighy

programme.title: Animal Farm (1954)
programme.desc: Animals rebel against their unscrupulous owner and take control of his farm, vowing to run things fairly, with equality for all. However, the pigs leading the revolt succumb to greed and corruption, culminating in violence and oppression. Animated drama based on George Orwell's novel, narrated by Gordon Heath and with Maurice Denham providing the voices for all the characters

programme.title: Big Stone Gap (2014)
programme.desc: A self-proclaimed spinster finds her life shaken up after learning a long-buried family secret. Romantic comedy, starring Ashley Judd and Patrick Wilson

programme.title: Eddie the Eagle (2016)
programme.desc: Biopic of British sporting underdog Eddie Edwards. Determined to fulfil his dreams of becoming an Olympic athlete, Edwards trains as a ski jumper - a field in which Britain had no other competitors. He secures a place in the 1988 Winter Olympics, where his can-do attitude makes him a media sensation in the face of a losing streak. Starring Taron Egerton, Hugh Jackman and Christopher Walken

programme.title: Mission: Impossible - Fallout Interview Special
programme.desc: Stars Tom Cruise, Rebecca Ferguson, Henry Cavill, Simon Pegg, Vanessa Kirby and director Christopher McQuarrie talk about making the sixth instalment in the franchise

programme.title: Behind Enemy Lines (2001)
programme.desc: A reconnaissance pilot is shot down and becomes stranded in enemy territory after filming evidence of a genocidal massacre by Serb militia in Bosnia. The naval air commander for Nato forces in the Balkans defies orders by mounting an unauthorised mission to rescue him, while the airman faces a battle to stay alive as he is pursued by paramilitary forces. Bosnian War drama, starring Gene Hackman, Owen Wilson, Gabriel Macht and Olek Krupa

programme.title: Chaos (2005)
programme.desc: A veteran cop and his inexperienced new partner are assigned to investigate a bank heist. At first sight, no money appears to have been stolen. However, it eventually emerges the criminal mastermind behind the robbery had a more complex and profitable scheme in mind - and a possible connection to the detective on his trail. Crime thriller, starring Jason Statham, Ryan Phillippe and Wesley Snipes

programme.title: Sword of Trust (2019)
programme.desc: A woman called Cynthia inherits a Confederate sword from her deceased grandfather. He believed it was proof that the South won the Civil War. However, when Cynthia tries to sell it to Mel, a pawn shop owner in Birmingham, Alabama, a wild adventure begins. Writer/director Lynn Shelton's comedy, starring Marc Maron, Jon Bass and Michael Watkins

programme.title: Entertainment (2015)
programme.desc: A middle-aged comedian plays a string of dead-end shows in the Mojave desert en route to meeting his estranged daughter. Drama, starring Gregg Turkington, with John C Reilly, Tye Sheridan and Annabella Lwin

programme.title: BSL Zone
programme.desc: A series of half hour programmes aimed at reflecting the interests, needs and aspirations of the sign language community

programme.title: Midnight Lace (1960)
programme.desc: American heiress Kit Preston is living a life of luxury in London with her new husband, but her idyllic life is shattered when she is terrorised by strange phone calls and voices in the fog. Her husband dismisses the threats as a practical joke, but as the scary incidents and menacing calls mount it becomes clear that someone really does have it in for her. Thriller, starring Doris Day, Rex Harrison, Roddy McDowall, Hermione Baddeley and Myrna Loy

programme.title: The Red Pony (1949)
programme.desc: A boy seeks solace from his feuding family through his friendships with his beloved pony and a ranch hand, whom he idolises. When the animal dies his relationship with his father seems to be damaged irreparably. Drama based on a short story by John Steinbeck, starring Robert Mitchum, Myrna Loy and Peter Miles

programme.title: Texas Lady (1955)
programme.desc: A woman arrives in Texas to take over the local newspaper, and vows to use it to expose corruption. Her first target is the gambler she wrongly holds responsible for her father's suicide, but who proves an unlikely ally when she faces the real villains - two cattle barons who rule the town with an iron fist. Western, with Claudette Colbert, Barry Sullivan and Gregory Walcott

programme.title: Run Wild, Run Free (1969)
programme.desc: Withdrawn, mute 10-year-old Philip is ignored by his parents and goes off to explore Dartmoor alone, where he meets a retired colonel who shares his knowledge of Devon's wildlife. Under the older man's nurturing tutelage, the boy begins to tame a wild white colt, but fate proffers a series of setbacks just as real progress is being made. Children's drama, starring John Mills, Mark Lester, Sylvia Syms and Bernard Miles

programme.title: The Imitation Game (2014)
programme.desc: Biopic of mathematician Alan Turing, whose work during the Second World War translated seemingly unbreakable Nazi codes as well as pioneering the development of computer technology. In the years following the war, his life would be destroyed as his homosexuality was discovered, leaving him facing criminal charges. Starring Benedict Cumberbatch, Keira Knightley and Charles Dance

programme.title: Mission: Impossible - Fallout (2018)
programme.desc: Ethan Hunt and the IMF team join forces with CIA assassin August Walker to prevent a disaster caused by mysterious arms dealer John Lark and a group of terrorists known as the Apostles. However, Hunt and his colleagues are soon pushed to their limits thanks to a face from their past. Writer/director Christopher McQuarrie's sixth entry in the action thriller saga, starring Tom Cruise and Henry Cavill

programme.title: The Florida Project (2017)
programme.desc: A six-year-old girl roams the grounds of the motel where she lives with her mother, looking for adventure with her small band of friends. Manager Bobby keeps a protective eye on the children, but his affection for them becomes strained as their mischief escalates. Drama, starring Willem Dafoe, Brooklynn Prince and Bria Vinaite

programme.title: Ida (2013)
programme.desc: A novice nun in 1960s Poland preparing to take her vows meets her last surviving relative, who discloses the fact her parents were Jews killed during the Holocaust. The revelation leads the would-be sister to investigate her family's past and the lingering impact of the genocide on her country. Oscar-winning drama, starring Agata Trzebuchowska and Agata Kulesza. In Polish

programme.title: Dark Command (1940)
programme.desc: A teacher is defeated in his ambition to become the marshal of his town during the early years of the American Civil War, so he turns renegade instead. Before long he has a reputation as a feared raider fighting on the side of the South, and it falls to the newly appointed lawman whose job he coveted to stop his reign of terror. Western, starring John Wayne and Walter Pidgeon

programme.title: The Black Tent (1956)
programme.desc: A wounded British Army captain is saved by a tribe of nomads in the Libyan desert during the Second World War, and they take him to their camp, where he is cared for by the sheikh's daughter and falls in love with her. Romantic drama, with Anthony Steel, Anna-Maria Sandri, Donald Sinden, Andre Morell and Donald Pleasence

programme.title: Two Thousand Women (1944)
programme.desc: Female prisoners struggle to cope with living in a German internment camp, but find common cause in making life difficult for their captors. When three British airmen are shot down over the prison, they keep them hidden from the Germans while plotting a way to smuggle them out safely. Second World War comedy, with Phyllis Calvert, Flora Robson, Patricia Roc and Renee Houston

programme.title: The Bridges at Toko-Ri (1954)
programme.desc: An American naval reserve pilot is called up for service in the Korean War, and must leave his family and civilian job as a lawyer. In command of a corps of naval pilots, he heads a bombing campaign on strategic Communist positions, but later comes to question the morality of the conflict. Adventure, starring William Holden, Grace Kelly, Fredric March and Mickey Rooney

programme.title: The League of Extraordinary Gentlemen (2003)
programme.desc: Fearless hunter Allan Quatermain teams up with a host of other characters from Victorian literature - including Captain Nemo, the Invisible Man, Tom Sawyer and Dr Jekyll - to put a stop to the nefarious Fantom's evil plot to bring about a world war. Fantasy adventure, based on Alan Moore's comic-book series, starring Sean Connery in his final screen role, Shane West, Stuart Townsend, Richard Roxburgh, Peta Wilson and Tony Curran

programme.title: American Woman (2018)

Director Jake Scott's drama, starring Sienna Miller, Christina Hendricks, Sky Ferreira and Kentucker Audley

Black comedy, starring Joan Plowright, Juliet Stevenson, Joely Richardson and Bernard Hill

Meanwhile, Ryder witnesses a robbery and discovers that his interview with the local news team has been turned into a video that has gone viral. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Man with a Plan
programme.desc: Adam recruits Marcy to put Andi's stuck-up sister Kelly in her place when she comes for a visit. Comedy, starring Matt LeBlanc

programme.title: Man with a Plan
programme.desc: When Adam and Andi see how much Lowell's relationship with Kelly is changing him, they stage an intervention to get him to stop putting up with her behaviour

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect looks back at some of the more ingenious Amazing Spaces builds where people have transformed the most unlikely types of spaces into incredible places to live and work - from a toilet converted into a luxury home to a Tube carriage transformed into a cutting-edge office. George also reflects on an air ambulance remodelled into a campervan, an old army truck turned into a family getaway as well as a double-decker bus reinvented as a Japanese-style pod hotel

programme.title: Ugly House to Lovely House with George Clarke
programme.desc: Two years ago, George meets Armin and Amifa, whose dark and dingy 1920s house in Bellingham, south east London, was not just ugly - the DIY extension added by former owners had been condemned and the makeshift kitchen was an accident waiting to happen. Architect Carl Turner did what he could with the £80,000 budget, but in the end the upstairs was left unfinished. George now returns to see how Armin and Amifa have tackled the top floor

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George Clarke meets Pip, a woman in West Yorkshire who is moving back into her childhood home, which was last decorated by her parents in the 1960s. Pip struggles with the idea of painting over her childhood wallpaper and replacing the kitchen that her father hand built, but George is on hand to offer her emotional support

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Sheffield, design and technology teacher Claire hopes her Asian menu will impress all of her guests but she's got a job on her hands with former-chef-turned-supermarket-worker Simon. Meanwhile, Helen's eccentricity irritates the others, including professional singer Ivor. It's an evening packed with spicy food, a dash of plasticine modelling and a sprinkling of offensive comments

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Design and technology teacher Claire hosts the second day of the competition in Sheffield, and hopes her Asian-inspired menu with a spot of clay model-making will impress her fellow diners enough to net her the points she'll need to secure the cash prize. However, former chef Simon Hollings proves difficult to faze, and the eccentric Helen irritates the other contestants with another audacious display at the dinner table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Service administrator Monika hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in Sheffield. She treats her guests to a traditional Bulgarian night inspired by her homeland, in the hope of impressing them enough to win the £1,000 prize money. However, some of the guests are unsure about her Bulgarian delicacies, and the host must fight for control when the two teachers go head to head and when Simon makes sexist jokes

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: After tensions arose between Claire and Helen on Monika's night, 1980s musician Ivor is determined to serve up some fun on his evening in Sheffield. He stages an '80s extravaganza, including a live performance from his new romantic band My Pierrot Dolls

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Former chef Simon hosts the final meal in Sheffield, but is unlikely to get an easy ride from his guests following all the criticism he has dished out at the earlier soirees. He also has to contend with the feud that's brewing between Claire and Helen, and when things boil over between the women, Monika doesn't know where to hide

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners of the musically themed Harmony B&B in Torquay, Devon, begin the competition, trying to prove their establishment offers the best value for money. Dutch hosts Hans MacDaniel and Monica van der Meij try to impress their guests, but it remains to be seen what they will make of wheelchair badminton, saxophones at breakfast and a controversial sausage

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest continues at Chimneys B&B in North Walsham, Norfolk, where owners Jenny and Richard Harmer's decision to use a flag as a tablecloth angers Hans MacDaniel and Monica Van Der Meij. The argument seems to abate during an afternoon canoeing session - only to resurface at breakfast the following day

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Beverley Conoley and Caroline Pocklington host at the Wordsworth Hotel in Cockermouth, Cumbria. They take their guests to a spa and Hans MacDaniel happily volunteers for a spray tan, while Richard Harmer is far more uncomfortable as he finds himself on a massage table. The day ends with dinner at a nearby bistro, but Jenny Harmer complains about the temperature

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mother and daughter Lesley and Emily Gray play host at the Park Farm Guest House in Gateshead, but while the evening's entertainment featuring a ride on a two-wheeled personal transporter goes down well among the guests, tensions simmer when the dinner conversation turns to a delicate issue

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hairdresser Julie hosts the first of a week of dinner parties in Co Durham, hoping to win over her guests with a no-nonsense menu she can prepare in advance. Joining her are tough-talking prison transport officer Tina, dreadlocked farm-dweller Stephen, superior IT manager David and glamorous beauty-school teacher Ashleigh. Alliances are quickly forged when David and Tina discover they both love a good gossip, before they turn their attention to Stephen when David compares him to a tramp. Julie overcooks her beef, but will her hosting make up for it and bag her the £1,000 prize?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Beauty teacher Ashleigh throws the second of the week's parties in Co Durham, she hopes a menu of sophisticated dishes - along with a helping of hearts and love - will help her win the cash prize. However, cracks were already beginning to show in the diners' genial facade yesterday, and the hostess realises she must play peacemaker if she is to keep everyone from falling out

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Call centre complaints officer and part-time farmer Stephen hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in Co Durham. He hopes to win the £1,000 prize money by impressing his guests with an evening on his farm, featuring a drinks reception and rustic cuisine. However, self-confessed snob Tina makes it perfectly clear what she thinks of his efforts

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Prison transport officer Tina is the fourth host in Co Durham. She hopes to impress her guests with a sophisticated French menu including salmon crepes, chicken and posh peas and pear dessert. But having been highly critical of everyone else's food so far, will they now take the chance to get their own back or can she win them round?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The final evening of gatherings in Co Durham sees IT manager David stage a French-themed soiree in the hope of securing the £1,000 prize. However, he soon finds his main challenge is to keep all his guests on friendly terms

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A woman who has taken drugs and is hallucinating causes a challenge at a hotel, and a worried mother rings for help when her child is feverish and unable to speak

programme.title: Titanic: A Dead Reckoning
programme.desc: Examining whether the sinking of the Titanic was also the scene of a crime, investigating whether the SS Californian could have done more to help the lives of those onboard

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A woman is airlifted to hospital after falling off her mobility scooter and getting knocked unconscious. Her daughter and husband discuss how she has led an active life despite being diagnosed with a crippling illness at a young age. A 78-year-old man is rushed into A&E after falling down the stairs, and doctors fear he may have a bleed on his brain

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The staff have a mystery to unravel when an unknown patient arrives after collapsing in the street. The team eventually manage to contact his daughters as they try to find out what may have caused him to fall unconscious. Meanwhile, a 29-year-old man comes in following an accident at the gym, and a three-year-old girl is in paediatrics with her mum showing signs of a chest infection

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Two motorcyclists need specialist care, as the helicopter medics try to save the life of Pete, while Tom may have a serious spinal injury. Plus, a woman is kicked in the face by her horse. Documentary following the work of air ambulance crews

programme.title: Titanic: A Dead Reckoning
programme.desc: Examining whether the sinking of the Titanic was also the scene of a crime, investigating whether the SS Californian could have done more to help the lives of those onboard

The team eventually manage to contact his daughters as they try to find out what may have caused him to fall unconscious. Meanwhile, a 29-year-old man comes in following an accident at the gym, and a three-year-old girl is in paediatrics with her mum showing signs of a chest infection

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The Craggy Island clerics face the prospect of an untimely death during the return flight from a visit to a holy shrine. Jack quickly commandeers the only parachute and bails out, taking the drinks trolley with him - leaving Ted and Dougal to their fate

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps Amber and Colin turn their bedroom into a dream vintage haven. After an initial assessment, the presenter begins by getting rid of any mass-produced furniture

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Music therapist Sarah Hadley from Surrey has a long-term love affair with the Greek island of Crete, a place she and her best friend Sue Williams have been visiting together for years. Now, Sarah is after her perfect Cretan holiday home and has asked Sue to help her with the decision. Jasmine Harman shows them five incredible properties for a budget of £150,000

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole restores two quad bikes and a stylish sun lounger, while Simon O'Brien rescues a holed canoe and turns an old oak beam into a spice rack

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Essex, where Henry restores a car trailer and a 1970s racing bike, and Simon turns his attention to lovingly restoring a vintage model aircraft

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate researchers take on the challenging task of finding the heir to a £200,000 fortune, and unearth the story of a tragic accident on the London, Midland and Scottish Railways in the process. Plus, a surprising family secret is uncovered in a case that took a team almost 20 years to solve

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A family leave London to run a gite in a tiny French village, while two best friends try to beat the competition as they open a bar on one of Spain's busiest seafronts. Plus, a couple of expats attempt make a success of their huge holiday complex in the middle of one of Europe's only desert regions

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at the White Lodge Hotel in Filey, North Yorkshire, where hosts James and Kim Hodgson hope their love of high standards will win over their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit is to the Old Red Lion Country Inn in Upper Poppleton, North Yorkshire, where pair Mark and Jean Lee hope their plain and simple pub B&B will impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest ends at glamping site Camp Katur in Kirklington, North Yorkshire, where Kerry Roy and Nicola Wilding hope to wow their guests with their unique 'geodomes' and see-through pods

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day of the contest arrives, and while Amanda Lucia-Charley and Alan Talbot receive harsh criticism of their breakfast, Mark and Jean Lee's decor receives scathing reviews

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: Investigating the case of a champion cat-breeder, and the grandsons of a Bristol man follow a trail that leads them to a secret about chemical weapons testing

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Spider monkey Kiara becomes a mother for the first time, but keepers worry that the baby is becoming weak as Kiara appears to be more interested in playing with the other monkeys than taking on maternal responsibilities. Meanwhile, baby capybara Henry enjoys having the attention of all the female capybaras, and Babirusa Sausu moves from paddock to paddock to mate with various females

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge steps in to help new chateau owners couple Erin and JB who, having recently moved into their fairy-tale chateau complete with turrets and a moat, have discovered rising damp. Dick goes on an exploration of their vast cellar in search of the leak

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud visits a house belonging to an engineer that resembles a giant snake. The building was inspired by an ammonite shell and has a scaly, curving structure that spirals over two levels and combines natural materials with the latest modern technology. However, completing the project within the 12-month schedule may not be possible for mechanical engineer Stephen Tetlow and wife Elizabeth

programme.desc: The recently discovered wrecks of German U-boats from the First and Second World Wars allow marine archaeologist Dr Innes McCartney to explore their dramatic stories

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Marie, 31, arrives at St George's A&E in south-west London by ambulance after collapsing at home with an unexplained headache. Doctors work fast to ascertain the cause of her collapse, with potentially devastating results. Marie's joined by her partner Cameron and her young child. Cameras also follow Eileen, 87, who comes in with shortness of breath and a high heart rate, and 10-month-old Erin, who has a build-up of wax in her ears following a cold and fever

programme.title: Food Unwrapped

programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp introduces new crafts, skills and techniques for interior decoration. In this edition, she reveals how to create a personalised vintage doormat and coasters, using spray paint, old tiles and serviettes

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Godsisters Roselyn Fleary and Jennifer Lawrence from London have always been best friends and holiday together every year on the Caribbean island of Grenada. As they both have family roots on the island they are now looking for a holiday home that could one day be a full-time retirement location. With a budget of £250,000 Scarlette Douglas shows them five properties, but can she tick all of their boxes?

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole head to Middlesex and Surrey, where projects include repurposing a 1950s hairdryer, updating a school desk and the restoration of a child's motorbike

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel the length of Britain turning unwanted junk into profit for their owners. In this episode they're on the south coast, where Henry gets to work an aircraft fuel tank while Simon gets obsessed with an iconic '60s speedboat

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The detectives search for the relatives of a well-liked Essex woman without knowing the value of her estate, and hope that their gamble will pay off when the amount is revealed . programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A couple welcome guests to their newly opened holiday complex in the Spanish desert, but a leak in the pool threatens to scupper their big plans. programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, keepers prepare for the difficult task of transporting giraffe Kidepo to a new zoo, and Kidepo's brother Narus is not making the move any easier. programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People

programme.title: Rick Stein's India

programme.desc: The final visit is to Villa Farm Cottage in Thornton Curtis in Lincolnshire, where perfectionist Petra Coates and her partner Martin Littlefield run a tight ship. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and settle some scores. Mark from the Bowling Green Hotel questions Martin and Petra about a lack of chairs and then gives Qidi and Andrew's noisy fridge comments a frosty reception

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Stephanie gets green-fingered as she sets out to restore the chateau's original greenhouse, which has recently been damaged in a storm. Lee and Belinda continue to expand their new outdoor venue. When their first wedding falls through, they fortunately get a new booking for a 60th birthday, but then end up in a race against time to get the garden party ready. With 12 acres of land to enjoy, Dick and Angel make sure their Wellington boots have a home, creating bootjacks for the newly renovated boot room

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Stephanie has a month to convert her old stable into a studio for the launch of her art weekends, but when she doesn't see eye to eye with mother Isobel about the design, renovations could take longer than anticipated. Belinda's promised a heated outdoor swimming pool on the chateau's website, so Lee starts constructing a solar panel system. Dick saves cash by getting creative on a new lighting project for Angel's treasury

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Boston to help change the fortunes of a failing Italian restaurant run by two brothers. The eatery's decline began when one of the pair started embezzling money to fund his prescription drug addiction, and things only got worse when the he was sentenced to four years in prison. With the time now served, he has returned, but must first regain his brother's trust before he can save their business

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Bridgeport, Connecticut, to visit Cafe Tavolini, which is being neglected by husband and wife managers teetering on the edge of divorce. The estranged couple's two children are struggling to save the business from their parents' disputes, and the visiting chef tries to shock the pair into stopping the restaurant from becoming a casualty of their quarrel

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Shane and Sharon dream of owning a holiday home on the Costa del Sol that they can escape to during the winter to enjoy the area's all-year round outdoor lifestyle. With a budget of £135,000, they want an apartment or town house close to the beach around La Cala de Mijas, with two bedrooms so their family can come to visit. Danni Menzies rises to the challenge to find them the perfect property

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Grandparents Alan and Elisa Bowers have lived in Plymouth for all of their lives and are itching for a new start, but are torn between moving to the beautiful Cornwall coast to stay close to family, or relocating to Lanzarote, their dream location. Laura Hamilton shows them three properties in Cornwall for their £300,000 UK budget while Scarlette Douglas has some amazing places in Lanzarote for their budget of £270,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Retired couple Dabi and Devi can't wait to wave goodbye to the grey skies of Croydon, south London, and head to the sunshine and heat in the seaside town of Gandia in Valencia. They're looking for a second home where they can spend up to six months at a time. With a budget of £180,000, Lee Juggurnauth lines up five impressive options for them to consider

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Retired bus driver Sue and postal worker Ali are relocating from Weston-super-Mare to inland Spain and hope Ben Hillman can find them a permanent home on their budget of £70,000

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect travels to Macclesfield to meet a first-time builder who hopes to transform an allotment shed into a Doctor Who-esque, multi-functional play den for less than £300. He also visits a spectacular live-in tree house in Scotland, and creates a medieval-style lock for the wood cabin, with Will's help. The Italian road trip takes in a holiday lodge made almost entirely from glass in the Dolomites

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Andy Stakes and Jeanette Hardy, who are building a new house on top of a sheer cliff on the west coast of Scotland. The pair want to spend £250,000 on a two-bedroom, glass-fronted and earth-sheltered home for themselves and their two beloved Newfoundland dogs. However, construction on such an exposed site is fraught with danger and the pair face no end of problems throughout the build, and a last minute design change that begs the biggest question: even if Andy finishes the house, will Jeanette actually like it?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on shows including SAS: Who Dares Wins, Dragons' Den, Three Families, Headspace: Guide to Sleep, Secret Crush, Lego Masters USA and This Morning. They also proffer their thoights on a BBC News report about taking holidays abroad as the pandemic rules on travel are relaxed

programme.title: SAS: Who Dares Wins
programme.desc: After a physically and psychologically intense introduction to their SAS selection, the obstacle for the 17 remaining recruits to overcome over the next 48 hours is fear, as they are sent deeper into the wild and unforgiving terrain of the Isle of Raasay in Scotland. They are put through their paces in a forward abseil, exposed to extreme cold in a water immersion exercise and required to operate as a team in a pitch-black disused tunnel

programme.title: Naked Attraction

Presenters Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding guide them through the assorted tasks, and Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith pass judgment on their skills. On the menu are scones, a devilish technical, and a showstopper challenge in the form of self-portrait croquembouches. Which of the famous faces will impress the judges?

programme.title: Johnny Vegas: Carry on Glamping
programme.desc: The comedian's attempts to create a glamping site made up of repurposed old vehicles hit more problems when master craftsman Pauleo tells him his 1950s Maltese bus is not worth saving. Johnny buys another vintage camper van and decides this one is a surprise gift for Bev - now all he has to do is keep the secret from her. Meanwhile, the pair head to Yorkshire to check out a possible location for the glampsite, only for the rise of Covid-19 to threaten the entire enterprise

programme.title: Davina McCall: Sex, Myths and the Menopause
programme.desc: Davina McCall was 44 when she began to experience hot flushes, depression and confusion - symptoms she hid until she discovered her body was starting the transition to menopause. Here she describes her own experience to bust the myths surrounding the menopause, tackling the mid-life taboos that can destroy women's jobs and relationships, and exposing the lack of specialist education for GPs and the confusion that still exists around hormone replacement therapy

programme.desc: Plumber Steve Capel hosts the second party in west London, putting together his favourite dish, a roulade of ostrich, kudu and springbok. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Ex-wedding planner and self-confessed perfectionist Zae Greenwood hosts the third dinner party in west London. He tries to impress his guests with a wedding-style menu, but they do not respond well to his culinary efforts

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Shane and Sharon dream of owning a holiday home on the Costa del Sol that they can escape to during the winter to enjoy the area's all-year round outdoor lifestyle. With a budget of £135,000, they want an apartment or town house close to the beach around La Cala de Mijas, with two bedrooms so their family can come to visit. Danni Menzies rises to the challenge to find them the perfect property

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Former pub landlady Janice Gibson wants to swap her Newcastle lifestyle for a new start in the Mar Menor area of southern Spain, so Laura Hamilton shows her a selection of properties starting from around £55,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sara Damergi helps Lesley Wall and Raspal Chima find a holiday home in Spain's province of Seville, with a view to spending half the year there when they eventually retire. With a budget of £150,000, the couple are amazed at how far their money will stretch

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Marketing manager Stephanie Hale hosts the first party in Nottingham, where her guests are local councillor Alex Foster, beauty therapist Janice Overton, project engineer Brian Hodges and businessman Barry Dickman. She encounters problems when one of the diners takes a dislike to her prawns, while another has trouble identifying an artichoke

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Local councillor Alex Foster throws the second party in Nottingham, hoping to impress with his French menu and a spot of bell-ringing for entertainment. However, one guest likens the host's starter to cat food, while another criticises his home's tidiness

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Beauty therapist Janice Overton hosts a 1970s-themed dinner party in Nottingham. The guests seem to be in the mood when they arrive suitably dressed for the occasion, but they soon begin to feel uncomfortable as one of them reveals that he is not keen on a fellow diner

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Engineer Brian Hodges hosts the penultimate dinner party in Nottingham. Knowing his skills are limited, he decides to rope in his local butcher to help out in the kitchen, borrows cutlery from a pub and persuades a friend's daughters to waitress for him

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Party animal Barry Dickman hosts the final dinner party in Nottingham, hoping his Greek-themed night will be enough for him to win the competition. His guests are served the country's traditional cuisine and are encouraged to dance, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Bridgeport, Connecticut, to visit Cafe Tavolini, which is being neglected by husband and wife managers teetering on the edge of divorce. The estranged couple's two children are struggling to save the business from their parents' disputes, and the visiting chef tries to shock the pair into stopping the restaurant from becoming a casualty of their quarrel

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Stephanie gets green-fingered as she sets out to restore the chateau's original greenhouse, which has recently been damaged in a storm. Lee and Belinda continue to expand their new outdoor venue. When their first wedding falls through, they fortunately get a new booking for a 60th birthday, but then end up in a race against time to get the garden party ready. With 12 acres of land to enjoy, Dick and Angel make sure their Wellington boots have a home, creating bootjacks for the newly renovated boot room

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Debbie and Steve have been visiting Fuerteventura every year for the past decade and now they want their own bolthole in the resort of Costa de Antigua. Jasmine Harman lines up five properties for their £80,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Scarlette Douglas lines up five stunning properties for widowed Maria, who wants to start a new life around the stunning lake and town of Viñuela in Málaga, on a budget of £250,000

programme.title: George Clarke's National Trust Unlocked
programme.desc: George discovers some of the UK's most impressive historic homes, as he is granted special access to some of these extraordinary places, delving into the hidden recesses that visitors don't normally get to see. In this first episode, George's journey begins in Dorset at the UK's most exquisite example of Italianate architecture - the magnificent 17th-century Kingston Lacy. He uncovers a tale of shame and scandal on the south coast, and the beautiful Cotswold landmark - Hidcote Manor Gardens - in full bloom takes his breath away. George also mines the history of the country's last cave dwellers at one of Britain's best-kept secrets, the Rock Houses at Kinver Edge, and finally, with his husky Loki, takes a walk at spectacular Studland Bay, part of the Jurassic Coast World Heritage Site

programme.title: Titanic: Into the Heart of the Wreck
programme.desc: Capturing the underwater odyssey of the greatest Titanic expeditions and the evolution of the most scrutinised shipwreck in the world. From the epic discovery of the wreck by a French-American team in 1985 to the exploratory missions led by James Cameron, who managed to light the wreck scene like a Hollywood film set and venture inside the ship thanks to remote-controlled robots, this documentary captures the unequalled thrill of those extreme dives

programme.title: Catching Paedophiles: Crime and Punishment
programme.desc: Film exploring the challenges of investigating and prosecuting child sexual abuse, and how to manage predators when they've been caught. Detective Claire Lyons, who works for Hampshire Constabulary's Internet Child Abuse Team, has to prove that her suspicions are right about a man who has been uploading indecent images of children - and not risk any mistakes that would lead to the suspect walking free and being able to offend again

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedic Rob and technician Mike rush to a 21-month-old baby who has fallen unconscious in Kidderminster, while two men collapse in a pub in Coventry

programme.title: The Money Maker
programme.desc: Entrepreneur and investor Eric Collins heads to Birmingham to visit frozen meal delivery company Winny's Meals. Shaun Sookoo started his Caribbean-inspired food company three years ago when he decided to turn his mum's home-cooked meals into a business and had friends queuing round to block to buy them. The business has been expanding at an exponential rate but when Eric looks at the figures, he is shocked to discover serious problems. Not only are customers no re-ordering, but Shaun's mother is not involved any more

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: A romp through the archive of the unconventional dating show, beginning with assorted 'oh my god!' moments, and catching up with some of the most famous participants. They include Judith, the breast cancer survivor from series five who played The Lord Is My Shepherd to the potential dates and made them a Victoria sponge

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help two couples searching in south London. Elliot and James want to buy their first place together but are struggling to pin down exactly where. Their favourite areas are Putney and Richmond, but their money does not go as far as they would like. Pam and Ross are in Wimbledon, where former tennis professional Ross works. However, having been stung after the 2009 housing crisis, they are understandably cautious

programme.title: The Great Garden Revolution
programme.desc: Joel Bird creates an incredible teaching shed with a front that's covered in habitats for wildlife, while Poppy Okotcha creates sensory beds and vertical planting that's perfect for any smaller garden. The team also visit the Hanifs, who live in the middle of Birmingham, but want to create a forest school that the whole community can benefit from

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Katie-Louise, who runs a buy-and-sell website for ex-pats, is on a mission to persuade businesses to donate for a charity auction to play out live on the radio. Meanwhile, Geoff, who has become the very first Mr Whippy of Benidorm, having bought a van and fitted it out with a soft ice cream machine, faces a potentially terminal problem when his generator blows up

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Another glimpse into the lives of expats who have left the UK for a new life in the Spanish sun. Cabaret star Mark - who appears on stage as Ruby Rox - films a video to play as part of his show, and sisters Vina and Nilam launch a business selling spice kits so expats can make their own curries. Tattoo artists Blane and Mark are in need of more customers, while retiree and metal detector enthusiast Stan spends his evenings searching the beaches for small change

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Another glimpse into the lives of expats who have left the UK for a new life in the Spanish sun. Former Glaswegian taxi drivers Pete and Kath came to Benidorm to retire, only to end up taking over a bar, while fitness instructor and entrepreneur Anthony has organised a campsite coach trip to the Algar waterfalls. Female impersonator James and his partner David have rented a working farm, and it proves a steep learning curve for the pair who have sunk every penny into the project

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: A 74-year-old with breathing difficulties is rushed into Barnsley Hospital, and registrar Dr Nick Mani considers placing the patient in a medically-induced coma to stabilise him. Junior doctor Ashleigh Trimble assesses 70-year-old Dave, whose daughter Sarah fears her dad's confusion and loss of appetite are masking a more serious condition

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Russell, a 72-year-old man is rushed into hospital by paramedics suffering from severe chest pains, while Dr Dyfrig Hughes treats Michael, who was brought in by ambulance after falling ten feet from a ladder while cutting hedges. Elsewhere, junior doctor George Kay has some exciting news to share with the team

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Doctors treat an elderly patient rushed into hospital suffering from a racing heartbeat, and a 28-year-old man receives treatment after a motorbike accident. Nurse Richard Jackson meets Graham, a patient with a complex medical history, who has been brought in with the symptoms of a stroke

programme.title: The Missing Files
programme.desc: This true crime documentary about the case of Milly Dowler looks in new detail at the role of the media, the police investigation into her disappearance and Levi Bellfield, the man who was eventually convicted of her murder. Why did it take police so long to look at Bellfield, who lived just yards from where Milly was last seen? And what was behind the tabloids' hacking of Milly's phone?

programme.title: Born to Kill?
programme.desc: A psychological profile of the former nurse, who is currently serving 13 life sentences for murdering four children and injuring nine others at Grantham Hospital in Lincolnshire in 1991. Psychologists and criminology experts explore the possible motives for her crimes, examining her behaviour during childhood, when she was known to be an attention seeker

programme.title: Murder in a Small Town
programme.desc: The re-examination of the case against Luke Mitchell unearths shocking revelations about two males who were identified by eyewitnesses at the scene where Jodi was murdered. The conduct of the police while interviewing Mitchell as a 14-year-old is called into question, and Luke and his mother Corinne take a lie-detector test

programme.title: When Kids Kill
programme.desc: Aaron Roach Bridgeman explores court restrictions and anonymity orders placed on child murderers, meaning the media cannot name perpetrators until the age of 18

programme.title: Murdered by My Daughter
programme.desc: Documentary examining the events surrounding the murders in April 2016 of Elizabeth and Katie Edwards by 14-year-olds Kim Edwards and Lucas Markham, Britain's youngest double killers. The officer in charge of the case gives an insight into the investigation, including the recording of the interview in which Kim explaining why she killed her mother and sister, and one in which her boyfriend detailed his involvement in the crime

programme.title: Filthy House SOS
programme.desc: Richard and Brennan are in Oldham, coming to the aid of 70-year-old part-time DJ Olly, a compulsive shopper, whose house is packed to the rafters with junk

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: In this edition officers have to deal with the consequences of cars hitting cyclists and pedestrians. In York, traffic cops need all their professionalism to deal with a distressing situation when a pregnant bike rider is knocked off her bike by a lorry, while a pedestrian in Leeds has been hit by a driver who appears to have used their car as a weapon. But does CCTV footage show the whole story? Jamie Theakston presents

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Officers are led on a chase through Bradford as they pursue a car stolen in Manchester by teenagers, while burglars who have been targeting pensioners are spotted in Halifax. Meanwhile in North Yorkshire, members of the road crime team have their hands full tracking thieves operating in a convoy of cars who steal from builders' vans in hotel and pub car parks. Jamie Theakston presents

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Barry Peach outsmarts a disqualified driver, handler Jon Peacock takes his new sniffer dog Jack to carry out a drugs warrant, Carl Warrener makes a jaw-dropping discovery when he pulls a car over in the early hours and Brian Conville gets mobbed when he deals with a reckless learner driver

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: PC Barry Peach hunts for a man wanted for assault, officers are hot on the heels of a suspected drug dealer, a drunken driver tries every trick in the book to avoid giving a breath sample and dog-handler Jon Peacock has a late-night encounter with two unusual law-breakers

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Dan Brigginshaw is kept on his toes when a suspect does a runner, a routine vehicle check turns into a drug bust for Suzie Ranyard, and Barry Peach has his hands full when a group of Scottish revellers clash with bouncers in Skegness. Elsewhere, Jim Woolerton and Jason Baxter catch out a rookie rider who is breaking the law

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: A mini-heatwave results in an influx of patients at Barnsley Hospital. 50-year-old Mark is rushed into the emergency department after an accident involving a garden shredder, while three-year-old Ophelia is brought in by her dad after a mishap with a sun cream bottle. Elsewhere, doctors treat 64-year-old Melvin, who suffers from the incurable lung condition COPD, and 27-year-old Jack is assessed after collapsing at work

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Bea discovers a massive file on Ned, and has flashbacks to Scarlett. Mackenzie decides to confront Toadie about his harsh treatment of Melanie, and to her surprise, he turns around

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Christian finds himself drugged, strapped to a bed, and faced with a murderous Lewis. Bella tries to find a quiet space to finish her assignment

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: Entrepreneur Damien Lee, his beauty consultant partner Weronika and sons James and Josh swap lives for a week with single mum Larissa Wood, her brother Conor, son Kobie and daughter Imahny. The Lee family live in a large Victorian house in Bournemouth, just minutes from the beach as well as their favourite organic food shops and restaurants, while 20 miles inland in rural Dorset lives the Wood family, whose home is a council house in the village of Winterborne Kingston

programme.title: William & Kate: Too Good to Be True?
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the relationship between the Duke and Duchess of Cambridge. programme.title: Secrets of the Mega Resort
programme.desc: Baha Mar has 11 swimming pools, 40 restaurants and bars, a 100,000 square-foot casino and its own squadron of trained sharks. Cameras take a closer look at one of the world's most luxurious resorts

Local heavy metal star Jonny Hellraiser tries to attract customers to his 1950s-style diner by introducing 'the world's biggest breakfast'. Business is hectic for wedding planner Louise, although she has not heard from her latest couple for weeks and has no idea how many guests are due

programme.title: Paddington Station 24/7
programme.desc: A vital piece of track-insulating equipment has failed, throwing train services into chaos, while a nasty surprise awaits engineers as they discover rotten wooden sleepers that disintegrate under foot. Plus, veteran staff are shocked by the reason the 15:00 to Bristol has come to a halt - a driver has tried to start the train from the wrong end, leading it to automatically shut down

programme.title: Filthy House SOS
programme.desc: Extreme cleaners Richard and Brennan travel to Gateshead to help 40-year-old sci-fi collector Abi, whose massive horde of toys, posters and films has completely taken over her home

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Officers have their hands full over the festive period as party-going drunks end up getting behind the wheel, while the stresses of surviving Christmas lead to late night argy-bargy and screaming matches. Jamie Theakston presents

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: An edition focusing on high-speed pursuits, revealing the judgement calls officers must make while weighing the dangers against the chances of capturing their suspects. On the M1, Karl hits speeds of 155mph to intercept a getaway car used by a gang of suspected thieves, but as he waits for other units to arrive his cover is blown and the target vehicle makes a run for it

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A foul-mouthed pensioner pushes Barry's patience to the limit, Sarah gives a bad-boy biker an early birthday present and Jim's Audi S3 is outclassed by a car 30 years its senior. Plus, Mike is left open-mouthed when he encounters a drink-driver who is three times over the limit

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jim is on the trail of a speeding motorcyclist, Jon reveals his guilty pleasure when he retrieves a stolen caravan and dog handler Barry is brought to book by a would-be lawyer. Meanwhile, Sarah deals with the aftermath of a drunken brawl between two brothers

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jim intercepts a car containing cannabis, a late-night reveller talks himself into trouble and Sgt Baxter has his work cut out when confronted by a dishonest driver. Plus, young drivers get a crash course in how to deal with skids at a training session run by the unit at RAF Scampton

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of medical emergency teams in the A&E department of Barnsley Hospital, beginning as severe weather conditions place it under even more stress than usual. Dr Julian tends to pensioner Brian who was brought in after breaking his ankle walking his dog in the snow, and the medic also sees former miner Andrew, who has a chronic lung condition. Paediatric consultant Dr Jane is called into action when four-year-old Teddy is rushed in after gashing his head in a sledging accident

programme.desc: A near-miss with Terese makes Toadie and Melanie realise they are going to have to be much more secretive with their affair

programme.desc: Cameras follow some of the people who have seen their houses and lives destroyed by the terrifying phenomenon of sinkholes

programme.title: Secrets of the Luxury Super Yachts
programme.desc: Documentary following the 'birth' of a new, 65-metre luxury vessel called Life Saga - designed, sold and captained by the best of British boaters. This expose of the secretive world of super yachts also spills the beans on how the super-rich really behave on board their boats, reveals how security teams employ hi-tech tools to defend against pirates, and features the brokers who shake hands on mega-million pound deals

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: Security are called to an incident in the Minors waiting room after a violent argument begins between two patients, while 47-year-old factory worker Mark arrives after an industrial accident tore the muscle and skin away from his wedding ring finger. Ian arrives in Resus with a worrying head injury after the 63-year-old fell down a full flight of stairs late at night and Dr Jehad must establish if he has a life-threatening bleed on the brain

programme.title: 999: Critical Condition
programme.desc: Helen has an aneurysm in her brain that could burst at any time and lead to her death within minutes. For doctor Sanjeev Nayak there are no second chances as he embarks on an extraordinary operation to save his patient. Michael's life is also on a knife edge. After collapsing with a cardiac arrest, he was saved by a neighbour giving him CPR. But now the trauma doctors face a double battle - they must quickly assess his head injury and also remove a clot in his artery that is destroying his heart
